* 3 2 1 \[Applause\] \[Music\] go this game's tier list can seem pretty set in stone sometimes but in reality major shifts in The Meta can happen extremely quickly especially when new resource hotpots and points of interest open up builds which are well adapted to capitalize on these flash points can see dramatic ascensions on the teist one such event occurred when humans unlocked the food storage ability this greatly improved the humans survivability by giving them a consistent source of XP even during harsh Winters or famines  
* that is if they could defend their stockpiles these stashes were incredibly valuable and could easily support entire colonies of rats and sparrows if improperly guarded and while these Neolithic humans did have some techniques they could use to try and protect their precious Grain on their own ultimately their Saving Grace came in the form of a previously low tier Predator build that was barely scraping by in the North African savannah the wild cat a build which once domesticated would go on to become one of the most  
* oppressive overpowered predators in the current meta but what is it about the house cat that makes it such a dominant threat and how was it that the wild cat became better suited at taking advantage of pest infestations than other builds to really explain the success of the house cat we need to go way back and start our story just after the myosin balance patch this update granted mammals ACC access to a new ability called carnal teeth a side grade of the dentici skill tree which allows users to gain bonus XP from carcasses and carryon  
* sources this may seem like a minor thing but it completely changed up the metag game and made hyper carnivorous builds a lot more viable giving rise to a new archetype called the carnivoran a group which includes many of the most popular Predator builds in the current meta carnivorans are divided into two factions caniforms and felifors caniforms are more generalist than nature and invest a lot into the old factioned skill tree letting them track the sense of whatever they need be it scavenge volaron prey fruit honey water  
* or teammates from a great distance they also tend to have a lot of stamina allowing them to run for long distances without tiring felifors on the other hand doubled down on the carnivoran archetypes original strategy they spec into even more abilities meant to crank up the efficiency of the hyper Carnivore Game Plan the ability tongue papill allows felids to extract even more value from fresh kills instead of investing heavily in to scent-based sensory power felifors tend to focus more on their hearing and eyesight abilities this  
* doesn't help them scavenge since things like carcasses and berries don't make sound but it does make them incredibly good at detecting hidden prey also in keeping with the carnivore's first major advancement felifors also focus a lot more heavily on Specialized dentition optimizing their bite attacks to deal massive puncture damage and more effectively hold on to prey so the user can deal bonus damage with their claws throw in a few more Ambush focused abilities like retractable Claws and camouflage fur patterns and you've  
* arrived at the Modern felid easily one of the most successful stealth assassin builds the game has ever seen in fact during the Ice Age expansion fids were such a dominant threat that many other Predator builds had trouble competing with them at all in the current meta cats remain incredibly dominant in virtually every major terrestrial server basically every cat including the ones I like to to throw shade at is a threat not just to those of similar weight class but even to builds that vastly outweigh them although their power stat  
* tends to be fairly modest among carnivorans through efficient use of critical hits a skilled fed player can take down just about anything while the majority of the fielded player base was off rampaging through the upper weight classes an offshoot of the faction opted to forego larger size and instead chose to spec into a few new abilities meant to capitalize on exploiting the weaknesses commonly found in the lower classes so let's finally start unpacking what makes the house cat so overpowered we'll begin with a quick look at the  
* cat's stats we've got a build with upper midlevel intelligence low defensive stats decent damage above average stealth High mobility and excellent perception right off the bat something that might jump out at you is well the fact that nothing jumps out at you cats are supposedly some of the most broken predators of all time and yet none of their stats seems all that busted on their own top tier builds tend to have at least one stat that is so High it completely invalidates the strategies of other players but no cats aren't able to  
* use tools they don't have a bone crushing or venomous bite they can't change colors they can't tank attacks for days they can't echolocate and so on so no obvious answer here next let's check out their unique abilities we talked about a few already including the abilities that buff the XP rates from meat sources these should not be understated as without them you can pretty much entirely write off the viability of this build cats and all other mammals have the warm blooded trait this grants them greatly improved  
* stamina regenerates greater resistance to cold damage and a bunch of other useful perks but at the cost of much greater upkeep requirements meaning they need to secure kills much more frequently than similarly sized cold blooded bills without these abilities granting the cat bonus XP from meat it would be very difficult for them to keep up with their energy needs even with how efficient cats are at hunting cats can also move extremely quietly due to their soft toe pads and retractable claws enabling them to easily get Within  
* striking range of prey through smart use of cover and line of sight but okay all types of cat large and small have those abilities how about something more unique to house cats for that let's look at their primary perception based ability slitted pupils this ability grants house cats extremely precise depth perception greater than all other feelers this allows them to perfectly judge the optimal pouncing distance massively increasing their odds of Landing an immediate critical hit and incapacitating their target before it  
* has a chance to respond so even though the house cat's bit damage isn't broken in and of itself when a house cat goes on the offensive it can certainly look that way this strategy is what has led to the complete collapse of several different player bases in the amphibian reptile bird and mammal factions as it truly is that oppressive now while we were first looking at the cat's base stats one thing you might have assumed right away was that the cat's lower defensive stats are its main weakness and that cats function as a sort of  
* glass cannon with very little counterplay options when attacked you might also assume that given the cats high Mobility their primary defensive strategy is simply to run away when they feel threatened and while their top speed is often enough to escape danger when needed instead cats employ their Mobility stat defensively in a different way this is where one of the most shockingly effective aspects of the house cat manifests house cats use their incredible agility to stand their ground agility and quick reflexes are  
* usually used to dodge attacks but cats instead actually use the reflexes to deflect them because their incredible depth perception grants them such high accuracy on their attacks they can use their claw swipe attack to actually block incoming strikes this is surprisingly consistent and works on a lot more attacks than you might think now obviously this is a high skill High reward strategy missing a Parry can result in serious damage but if you get good enough at this you can ward off attacks from just about anything and  
* potentially even open up opportunities for counterattacks put all this together and you come to understand what a house cat build truly is a house cat is what you get when you fuse the deadliness of a snake with the energy of a mammal but why does this play style make for such a good partnership with human players compared to dogs they seem like they'd be much less useful party members humans broke the game and became the top tier build by using coordinated team strategies and Tool use in order to utterly trivialize their matchup versus  
* large mammals dogs easily slot right into this game plan as they can help track and Chase down large prey and also sniff out any potential Ambush predators that could disrupt and punish this strategy when a successful kill is made dogs and humans naturally split the reward and both benefit dogs are also great defensively as they can keep watch at camp and alert party members of approaching enemies cats are generally pretty useless in this regard they don't have anywhere near the endurance required to chase down prey over long  
* distances can't Square Off head-to-head with those builds anyway and aren't nearly as reliable for early enemy alerts the kills a cat makes can't realistically be shared with humans either it wasn't until Humanity unlocked the agriculture ability that the player base began to notice additional vulnerabilities they didn't need to care about before vulnerabilities that dogs couldn't really help with when operating off of a hunter gatherer strategy humans could basically ignore all builds in the low weight class rats and birds were no  
* threat to humans and also not worth the effort to take down so it makes sense for them to just focus on larger quaries however when the focus shifts to farming and food storage those same builds humans were ignoring before suddenly became one of their biggest threats having a granery rated and its food reserves destroyed could easily wipe out an entire human settlement so figuring out proper security for these storage sites was critical dogs simply are not effective enough at stopping pest infestations to fill that role they  
* cannot climb or fit into small spaces and aren't stealthy enough to catch the trickier pests cats on the other hand are truly excellent at this task and come with other benefits as well main one being that they require very little upkeep although they are social builds they hunt alone so they don't require any direction or attention from their human party members in order to accomplish their task since humans don't have any need for rat or bird carcasses the fact that cats don't usually share their kills is actually a benefit rather  
* than a drawback the only thing cats really require from humans is to share in the shelters they construct in the wilderness cats typically use Burrows Hollow stumps or caves as their home bases but these are vulnerable to attacks human settlements are significantly safer speaking of safety even though cats are extremely lethal Hunters they pose very little danger to humans this is a huge advantage over snakes which theoretically could ally with humans to serve the same purpose but in practice are far too dangerous to  
* risk keeping around the fact that cats are one blooded and therefore need to eat more frequently than snakes is also a benefit rather than a drawback for humans since it means they need to stay on task and keep the guard up so while it is common to see players debate which build makes for the better addition to a human player's party the correct answer is actually both dogs are a huge help in the large creature matchup while cats cover your weaknesses in the small creature matchups having both in your party covers all of your weaknesses and  
* enables human settlements to use a hybrid play style of both hunting and farming so if you're a human being trying to construct the most optimal party comp the only real question is how you'll keep the dog and cat players from fighting each other now knowing which traits and abilities to invest in can be a difficult Choice luckily for you I've partnered with the game s to Singularity to provide you the perfect training simulation Sela Singularity is a scientifically accurate non-fiction Idol clicker game where you begin as a single  
* cell and have to unlock traits to evolve your biology increase your intellect and invent new technology in order to conquer the universe and reach the ultimate Singularity point it's a relentlessly fun Idol game that had me hooked for weeks I love the Charming low poly 3D animals that you unlock as you progress through the Tree of Life in fact I like this game so much that we actually decided to partner up and build an entirely new limited time game expansion focused entirely on exploring the adaptations that allowed cats to  
* conquer the planet it's free to play and available on Steam IOS and Android we worked closely together on this cat event so see if you can recognize and remember some of the important special abilities that I mentioned as you play through it if you do manage to get far enough in this event you'll even unlock a special tiu themed badge which will permanently give your simulation a valuable speed buff so please do check this one out because I genuinely have never been so excited for a brand partnership before this one really is  
* special to me and I guarantee you'll have fun playing selda Singularity again it is completely free to play so click or tap on the link in the description or scan the QR code on screen now thanks again for watching and as always good luck out there \[Music\] \[Applause\] \[Music\]

# 탭 23

* This episode was sponsored by Skillshare Human beings: Are they as overpowered as the game's community would lead you to believe? Short answer: yeah. They're the most meta-defining build of all time. And the undisputed top build in the current expansion But, believe it or not, this wasn't always the case. Humans used to get bodied left and right, on a daily basis by other players And it took a pretty long time before the player base how to actually make use of the human build's unique abilities The story of the humans rise to dominance is a really interesting one  
* And so today, we're gonna do an in-depth look at the human's abilities, their strengths, and their weaknesses Because yes, they do have weaknesses We'll also go over notable matchups and finally look at the steps human players took in order to reach their dominant status First, their unique abilities I touched on this topic in my video about primates, but I'll recap here And also go into more detail First off the bat, we have their ability to throw things.  
* Other builds can throw things, but humans can do so harder, faster and further than anyone else The reason for this is their body structure Bipedal builds are tough to balance Both within the game development sense and the center of mass sense. Throwing an item with any real force behind it requires serious balance Achieved only by having shorter arms and longer legs This is why humans can launch projectiles at deadly speeds Whereas other primates kind of just lob things without putting much effort into it  
* If they tried to, they would knock themselves over. Even if chimpanzees could figure out how to craft a spear, there's no way they could throw it hard enough to deal any significant damage Next, there's stamina regeneration. Humans can chase down anything If you try to run from a human, you might outspeed them initially, But you'll run out of stamina much sooner than they will.  
* And they'll catch you while you're in your weakened state. This is partially because of their superior balance again, But mostly it's because of the human build's unique choice to not spec into fur This gives them access to the extremely broken ability: sweat Every other current build that's decent at distance running recharges stamina by using the move "pant" This works great, but it does require the user to stop running Sweat, on the other hand, works better while moving Massively extending the distance that human players can run  
* They've got a few other important abilities that are essential to their kit But aren't unique to humans Their ability to speak is unique among mammals, but some birds can pull it off too. In humans, the ability to speak allows them to use one of their signature moves: teach, which drastically boosts skill point gain.  
* Mitigating the main penalty a player receives upon getting a game over Humans and birds also share another unique trait which is being able to stand upright. Standing upright is extremely useful for dealing with stealth builds that might normally be using tall grass to hide Tool use also might be thought of as a human-exclusive move, And it certainly is the most effective in humans But it turns out, that a pretty wide array of other builds can also do it.  
* Birds, primates, cephalopods and even fish can use tools on occasion But sweating and throwing, uniquely human. Now onto their weaknesses. And yes, it's true that currently most human players can subvert these with their crafting ability But it's important to recognize that without equipment, the human build isn't necessarily unstoppable The decision to not have fur granted humans a massive buff to mobility at the cost of stealth In the mammal faction, fur is the prerequisite to all the major stealth abilities camouflage  
* And lack of stealth abilities means getting in close on their targets is a serious challenge The human build's lack of fur also means that they have very low defense, both from physical attacks and from environmental affects. Bare skin offers almost zero armor To the point where even lower weight classes Have no trouble dealing damage to humans.  
* And bare skin also takes quite a bit of damage from the sun too. Humans are also pretty limited when it come to the power stat. Physically, they're the weakest of the great apes. And don't really have any good options for dealing damage They lack the classic high damage bite attack that nearly every other non-herbivorous build relies on, instead having to resort to blunt attacks like punch and kick.  
* Which we all know aren't very effective against builds that have thick fur or skin. The last major weakness that I can see is that they're pretty bad at dealing with stealth. The lack of night vision means darkness can really hamper their ability to locate targets And their lack of a strong sense of smell means camouflage is especially useful against them.  
* Okay, now let's talk about matchups. Obviously, a key feature of the human build is being able to craft equipment Given basic equipment, like low level armor and hunting gear. The human's best matchup is going to be against medium-large mammals Unlike blunt damage, piercing damage is quite effective versus builds with thick hide.  
* But on defense, they're actually pretty awful. A humans only option on defense is against a large mammal is intimidation. Unless they've got some crazy high level equipment But I'll make a video on power creep later. The classic equipment cannot stop a charging tank main Unless the intimidate attempt works.  
* But overall, this matchup is one of the reasons humans became top tier. Hardly any builds have favorable matchups against tanks. But that's not to say that humans don't have their counters though As I said before, stealth can be really effective against them due to their lack of a keen sense of smell and night vision.  
* Humans have the ability to win pretty much any fight as long as they've got time to prepare But, taken by surprise, humans have lot fewer options Another thing humans have trouble dealing with is flight. Even when a human has a ranged weapon, players with high aerial mobility are pretty safe during flight Now as far as I know, there aren't any bird builds powerful enough to one shot humans, which is important because blunt attacks like punches are actually quite effective against birds Birds have hollow bones that are resistant to tension and torsion, but not compression  
* This is the opposite of mammal bones, which are resistant to compression and tension, but not torsion. The point is, birds aren't the best choice against humans, but, flight has been unlocked by four different classes in the game Birds, which we just discussed aren't a good choice, bats, which do even less damage than birds.  
* Reptiles, which unfortunately had their arial builds banned in in patch 1.3.1 and insects Flying, eusocial insects have, by far, the best matchup versus humans in the entire game. Humans have zero counterplay options against a swarm of stinging insects. I mean, sure, there are special items that they can use to combat swarms if they're prepared.  
* But the amount of human mains who've chosen that as one of their prestige abilities is pretty small. Humans lack of fur means they have no innate defense against stings. Stings also have the chance to proc the anaphylaxis debuff, which can actually lead to a game over And it's for this reason that the high score for human eleminations Belongs to the africanized honey bee, known better by its community given name, the killer bee.  
* And even if the stings don't proc the allergic reaction effect, They will still definitely achieve their primary goal of dropping their resolve to zero and granting you control over the territory And we can see that lately the viability of killer bees has only been growing now that they've expanded their territory outside of Africa Which brings me to my main point, Are humans overpowered? Well, just like the africanized honey bee, Humans only really became OP once they escaped the African server, and invaded other areas.  
* Notice how humans took down megafauna across every terrestrial server except Africa The African tank mains have the matchup experience and counterplay options to deal with the vast array of skills humans have access to. But the rest of the community was totally unprepared to deal with the rapid invasion of new human mains invading their competitive scene.  
* In other words, the devs could have prevented the massive destabilization of the current meta if they'd region locked humans Staying competitive in the meta requires a good player constantly be leveling up their skills. There's no better example of this than the rapid rise to dominance that humans experienced If you would like to level up your own skills, and unlock new ones, I highly recommend skillshare Skillshare is an online learning community With over twenty thousand classes in design, business, technology, and more.  
* Premium membership gives you unlimited access to high quality courses on must know topics, so you can improve you skills unlock new opportunities and do the work you love. I especially love their course on motion graphics, which is taught by the folks behind In a Nutshell Join the millions of students already learning on Skillshare today with the special offer that's just for my subscribers.  
* You can try Skillshare for free, for two months by using the link in the description Or, go to skl dot sh slash tier zoo three. Act now for this special offer and start learning today. And special thanks to my patrons on Patreon. If you'd like to see More from me, my patreon and social media, are in the description.

# 탭 24

* you made it halfway stop them the mission area is yours four enemies spotted it's now or never stop them they captured the mission area well you can't expect to win them all the hyena may be one of the most misunderstood characters in the entire game hyena mans get flamed constantly with insults like cowardly underhanded and stupid when in reality they're one of the most interesting powerful and effective characters of them all they have several abilities which grant them unique advantages seen almost nowhere  
* else in the game and a stat spread that is undoubtedly very unorthodox for a member of a carnivorous mammal faction so today let's examine the stats and abilities of the hyena to get a better sense of their true strengths and weaknesses and ultimately decide where they fall on the tier list first let's do a quick overview of how exactly the hyena became such a popular character to pick in the current meta perhaps one of the most counterintuitive facts about the hyena is that the hyena is a Fela form meaning it is more closely related to builds  
* like lions and tigers not wolves or Bears this is strange because clearly in terms of the majority of their basic traits the hyena is very canine likee there are a few different subtypes of the hyena build but this video is going to focus mostly on the spotted hyena simply because it is by far the most competitively viable but I will discuss the other subtit ypes later on in the video next Let's do an examination of the spotted Hyena's base stats to see if we can pick out any obvious standout strengths or weaknesses and right away  
* one thing we can spot is just how high the Hyena's power stat is the Hyena's bite force is one of the highest in the game especially when you account for size sure the NY crocodile and hippopotamus have more powerful bites but they're all several times larger than your average hyena but for a midsize carnivorous mammal the hyena is a huge outlier in terms of its bite force moving on to Mobility this one's a bit tricky Mobility is their second highest stat and indeed hyenas are quite excellent Runners if viewed in a vacuum  
* but taken in proper context it is a bit more of a mixed bag the hyena actually has one of the lowest top speeds of any carnivorous mammal build in the African Savannah server now top speed isn't the end all Beall of Mobility because of a special ability we'll discuss later the Hyena's stamina is effectively doubled compared to that of similarly sized Predators allowing it to chase other players for a greatly extended distance now because of its lower top speed it usually cannot CL CL the distance on its own during Pursuit but a lot can happen  
* during an extended Chase and there's very little room for error when being chased by hyenas still having an unimpressive top speed does present some vulnerabilities in the hyena strategy in addition to potentially getting dusted by your hunting Target during a Chase having weaker burst Mobility options gives you less opportunity to retreat should an opponent decide to Counterattack this makes it tougher to pressure opponents who are standing their ground as you can't weave in and out of their attack range as safely and  
* could easily get hit for serious damage if you aren't careful which brings me to their next stats HP and defense hyenas exhibit surprisingly High toughness in combat being able to walk off a decent amount of damage still given just how insanely stacked the African meta is even with above average tankiness there's still very little room for mistakes in combat they're still nowhere near as durable as say the honey badger the gold standard for absurdly high defense stats in the African server still got to give credit where it's do  
* hyenas can handle your average scrap just fine which is kind of necessary due to their next stat their intelligence is hard to place on one hand they are excellent pack Hunters able to coordinate hunts and attack from multiple directions this offsets the disadvantages of their lower movement speed a bit and also makes their attacks very hard to defend against alone however hyena Clans are also some of the least safe teams to be on with Friendly Fire being uniquely commonplace now most teams experience at least some dueling  
* between clan mates but typically this is confined either to specific seasonal events like the rut or as a result of a new party leader securing dominance with hyena the fights are fairly constant especially after a successful hunt as party members must then compete for loot this stands in contrast to other top predators of the server like wild docks which have a much less competitive process to split loot so it's surprising that a build with such high intelligence is prone to as much Friendly Fire as say a rhinoceros for stealth hyenas do have  
* some fairly decent camouflage but this is almost entirely negated by how much noise hyenas make hyenas are notoriously loud not only in their vocalizations their signature laughs and chirps but also their movement their running gate is a sort of gallop which is significantly louder than the careful steps of other fofor Predators couple that with their lack of retractable Claws and they really just have a very difficult time concealing their presence as they approach hunting Targets in fact among their competition hyenas are more  
* likely to get ambushed themselves than actually pull off a surprise attack their perception abilities are pretty typical nothing too out of the ordinary in this department they aren't all that good at detecting hidden players but do have an ear for sensing commotion that they might be able to jump into their sense of smell is also acutely attuned to being able to detect Carion aiding them in their quest to scavenge next the Hyena's special abilities there are two main ones the first is the Hyena's disease resistance hyenas are seconded  
* only to vultures in their ability to ignore poison damage from bacteria and viruses now disease resistance is pretty much always useful it increases the character's chances to recover from injury and lowers the chance of just getting one shot out of nowhere by a random Illness but having a supercharged immune system is an expensive ability that comes with some downsides too so this needs to serve a dual purpose and in this case it's pretty obvious this ability dramatically expands the options they have to scavenge from for most  
* carnivorous characters the window for Scavenging a carcass closes pretty quickly especially under the heat of the African Sun careless players can easily incur the food poisoning debuff if they aren't careful picking at Old remains hyenas can get away with being a lot less cautious in this regard this synergizes extremely well with their High bite damage which enables them to crack open and digest bones a trait which puts them into a pretty exclusive Club of builds which are able to reliably access marrow as a form of  
* sustenance when it comes to large bones it's pretty much just humans hyenas and crocodilians that are actually able to crack them their second ability is their massive heart which is roughly double the size of similar carnivorous builds this grants them effectively twice as much endurance as they otherwise would have which helps offset their lack of speed outright even if they can't outspeed their targets they're usually able to outlast them and catch them once they run out of stamina there's not too much else to say about this ability it's  
* pretty straightforward but one thing I thought might also be interesting is to talk about which abilities the hyena does not have since I think that can often times be just as informative as to which advantages and disadvantages they exhibit as talking about which abilities they do have one of the most striking absences is retractable claws hyenas are part of the fofor faction meaning that if they wanted to spec into that ability it is available to them in fact before they reallocated their evolution points  
* into higher bite damage and disease resistance they actually did have retractable claws which likely would have given them higher stealth the ability to climb and higher slashing damage but for whatever reason the most popular strategy for the Proto hyena build needed all the help it can get in terms of run speed so keeping the claws unretracted as a means of traction was a worthwhile sacrifice another interesting absence is a tail hyenas have extremely short tails which locks them out of a lot of potential abilities now obviously  
* I'm not going to rag on hyenas too hard for not having a pentile tail or anything like that I a human M myself so it's not like I don't understand the upside of sacrificing access to tail based abilities but still among carnivorous builds in the African Savannah taals can be useful counterweights that provide stability during during a Chase lowering the chances that a player might trip and fall if they take a wrong step it's interesting one of the benefits to not having a long tail is just that it's one less thing that other players can attack  
* given that Hyena's heavily favor attacking the back end of a Target and even had been known to bite off Tails I have to wonder if hyenas simply have been opting for shorter taals just so that they don't get griefed by their fellow hyena Mains next let's talk about their matchups given that they compete for the same prey the most important match up for hyenas is the lion matchup unfortunately even though hyas have a much higher bite force in 1 V1 combat the Lion's reach advantage and size Advantage are too powerful resulting in  
* this being an almost completely one-sided match up a few paw swipes comboed into a bite to the neck will Zero to death a hyena basically every time and there's not much counterplay a hyena has alone especially given that the lion is able to Sprint faster over short distances so the hyena can't even run away hyena Mains are often depicted as thieves and while they do often steal kills when they get a chance when it comes to Lions it's almost always the other way around lion players love to score free loot by chasing away hyenas  
* and unfortunately even with the whole Squad together it only takes a few Lions to drive them away now not All Is Lost in this scenario this is actually one of the situations where the bone crushing bite is most useful as there's still lots of bone marrow left after Lions finish looting the kill that only hyenas can access but still this matchup is rough and it's hard to even imagine what ways the hyena could respect in order to challenge the lion lions are really the only problematic matchup though hyenas go pretty even with leopards with the  
* only real pain point being the hyena lack of ability to climb leopards exploit this by dragging their kills up into trees where hyenas can't contest them but this also puts the leopard in a pretty vulnerable position and if the hyena Clan is large enough they can essentially trap the leopard player and as for cheetah I mean do I really even need to say anything they pretty much have to avoid hunting anywhere near hyenas otherwise their efforts are all but guaranteed to be in vain cheetah have essentially no options when it  
* comes to defending their kills from hyenas and they can't climb either so there's nowhere to stash their loot for later so with all this being said where do hyenas belong on the tier list this is where some distinction between the different hyena variants is necessary the striped hyena and brown hyena both have a lower power stat and Mobility stat than the spotted hyena and focus more heavily on Scavenging instead they put more points into defense and perception aiding them in finding Carion far away ultimately I consider these  
* builds to be a lot less viable as they have to rely on griefing noobs and janitor in order to get by I would rank them in SE tier the spotted hyena however is a much different story it is legitimately one of the most powerful builds in the entire African metag game and can hold its own amid some intense hostilities its special abilities enable it to subvert some of the downsides that come with sharing territory with a larger Predator however much as I am tempted to be a contrarian and give them the rank of s tier I don't think

* saying all that much while it's nowhere near the level it needs to grant them tool use or problem-solving capabilities it does at least grant them low-level social capabilities their power stat is slightly below average which again is higher than most lizards this is pretty much just due to their large size which allows their basic klaustreichs tail swipes and bites to deal more damage in smaller lizards their best offensive move is tail whip which we'll talk about in the fifth the iguana bill doesn't possess any special offensive abilities  
* which makes sense given that the game most of their experience through foraging rather than PvP on defense iguanas have the reptile faction standard offensive bonuses from their scale attribute which negates all damage below a certain threshold and offers a high defensive bonus against /type damage while being vulnerable to bashing and piercing attacks this offers better protection than soft skin or fur but less effective than exoskeletons or shells in terms of mobility players are often surprised at how effective the  
* iguana builds burst movement options can be while their basic walking speed is deceptively slow the Gowanus can sprint short distances quite quickly however one of the reptile factions signature weaknesses is their low maximum stamina due to their three chambered heart as a result this burst of speed can only be used a few time's over short distances and even then these bursts of speed aren't always fast enough to escape more mobile builds like dogs or Raptors however in terms of versatility iguanas have a lot of lumen  
* options not just for moving on land but for arboreal and aquatic zones too iguanas can climb quite effectively and have no trouble swimming and this is all without wasting evolution points on abilities like cling pads or webbed feet which require sacrifices in other stats their claws make for effective enough climbing tools and their tails beg for great paddles meaning there are very few environments that are downright unplayable for iguana Mane's in terms of HP iguanas are again better than most lizards but still not exactly top of the

* they could be tilted towards the Sun to grant a cold resistance buff and can be flushed with blood to grant a heat resistance buff this versatile ability can allow would want us to thrive in a variety of environments from jungles to deserts to islands the Duke left second ability is that it can be used for intimidation by flashing their brightly colored dewlap they can inflict an intimidation debuff on an aggressive or overly curious nearby player this is essential to the iguana's game plan since its actual compatibilities are rather limited the  
* iguana's tail is integral to the iguana's combat moveset the move iguana players favor the most in battle is tailless because the iguana's tail comprises about half of their total body length this attacks have deceptively long reach this combined with its extremely low startup leg makes it an excellent selling tool that allows the iguana to control space quite effectively while it deals relatively low blunt-force damage it does also have a chance to inflict a stump giving the iguana player a chance for escape combat  
* if need be this attack does have a lot of end leg though meaning that if the attacking player can bait out or dog a swing gb1 is left wide open for a counter-attack overall though this attack serves its purpose decently well at keeping away rush down builds like dogs cats and birds this attack punishes players who approach Caroline and forces opponents to respect the iguana space however because of this attack slow direct damage players running high HP bills may elect to just tank the hip and continue their approach usually going  
* for a grab in this situation the iguanas only option is to use their other tail based ability potahto me this ability costs a large chunk of the iguana players max HP to act and can only be used once but functions as one of the best escape options in the game the user drops their tail and uses it as a distraction to flee if the attacking player falls for the bait this can save the iguana player from certain defeat however some builds particularly those with a higher intelligence level may be able to resist the distraction and  
* continue to pursue the player so it's not a sure fire escape despite its high cost personally I really dislike this ability especially on the iguana many lizard maidens use this ability to great effect but because do you want to relies so heavily on their tail I personally put opted for a different defensive option because using Auto Tony also renders tailwhip unusable without a tail they'd wanna are forced to fall back on they're much less effective bite and scratch attacks neither of which I'd consider much of a threat in combat overall the  
* iguanas combat strategy is solid in most situations but can completely fall apart against players who either know the matchup for have the right resistances to the iguanas abilities so I think it's safe to say that iguanas definitely aren't overpowered still they're undoubtedly higher on the tier less than average lizards because they've great matchups against several relevant builds particularly cats the basic green iguana I think belongs in beach here below the more oppressive builds in the meta but well above low \- you're trash like frogs

# 탭 26

* this episode was sponsored by Skillshare even though no individual insect can claim the number one spot on the tier list over the human build insects as a whole are easily the most successful build type in the history of the game the average insect ranks much higher than the average mammal or bird and this has been true even since before the devs added dinosaurs into the game I planned to make many insect tier lists in the future but I'm making this video now because rumors have started to circulate about leaks that data miners have found  
* concerning new unannounced balance changes to the insect class today I want to talk about the content of this discovery and its implications both for the insect player base and for the meta as a whole out sides community got understandably freaked out when data miners discovered a new hidden bounce patch titled insect pocalypse our insects being banned are insects being nerfed is it all insects or only specific builds well lucky for you guys tier zoo is here to give you the scoop on the insect pocalypse balance patch in  
* its entirety first things first no the insects pocalypse is not a universal balance change that affects all insects builds most of the nurse and buffs \- there are a few of those center around changes the overruled itself which directly affects insect mates but anyways let's discuss which insect builds are going to be most affected there are two main groups affected the first of which is the specialist builds many insect players have an extremely refined play style everything about their gameplay is highly specific the  
* only specific plants prey upon specific players and reproduce only in the exact right conditions naturally these players are much more vulnerable to having their game plans disrupted and well there's a lot of disruption going on because of that many of these strategies are becoming unbel obviously there are millions of potential examples so I can't talk about everything but one example most players will recognize is monarchs are highly selective in their early game XP sources during their early game they feed exclusively on milkweed  
* which makes sense because this grants them an important perk poison they can't exactly spec into a generalist build without sacrificing their only line of defense so they're in a tough spot as milkweed becomes rarer in game and gets cleared by humans to plant crops the second type affected the worst are builds like mayflies dragonflies and stone flies these all share one thing in common the first two levels of their playthrough are aquatic they start off the game in a river pond or swamp and have to level up by hunting there before  
* they reach the level where they unlock wings and leave their aquatic biome the aquatic biomes has become a lot tougher to survive in if you've got a sensitive respiratory system as it's become increasingly toxic mostly due to runoff robust fields like catfish can tank the added pollutants just fine but fragile nymphs don't have that ability and so build that have to lay eggs in water are seeing a decline now for the bills that will do well you probably guess that if specialists do poorly generalist insects will do extremely  
* well and you'd be right honestly I wouldn't expect generalist insects to experience much difficulty from this patch at all builds that aren't picky about their XP sources we'll still be able to find their way and should see their player bases remain relatively stable builds like the cabbage white butterfly a generalist butterfly Milligan and the ladybug a generalist fetal build are examples of great choices to survive the update especially the ladybug because it's got a mild poison that deters birds this poison doesn't require a specific diet  
* like the Monarchs does and considering Birds have a lot less potential targets now that many of you have aquatic insects are growing out of the meta this protection will be even more but there are some insects that have even been buffed in this patch and these are the scavengers same perks as generalists but also don't require access to the shrinking meadow biomes that most insects depend on in fact most of these builds do better in cities there are a few obvious examples that I'm sure you're well aware for example  
* cockroaches roaches have been skyrocketing up the tier list ever since they first expanded out of their African home server back during the colonial meadow but the insect buff the most in this patch is the Yellow Jacket Yellowjackets have been spiking in population lately due to milder winters and drier springs and they're perfectly suited to human dominated environments such as cities and parks Yellow Jackets have great DPS mobility and defense for their size and function super effectively as generalists they can also  
* hunt pretty effectively from the air and take on other players outside their own weight class solo and take on players far outside their weight class in groups they can build their bases anywhere and love to eat the scraps other players leave behind a trash filled world is a paradise for Yellowjackets and considering how quickly landfill biomes are expanding it's no surprise that they're growing in numbers too a quick lightning round for some other insect builds their respawn point the meadow is becoming more scarce as farmland biomes  
* replaced them but in most places their populations are doing fine although in America the variant that had the swarm ability actually got banned mantises are not picky when it comes to what they'll catch while they're still nerfed by the reduction in forest violence mantises along with spiders are still the most effective stealth build for that weight class their success depends on how badly the nerves affect number other flying insects that they come across since many of these insects are part of the category hit by aquatic nurse they could  
* potentially be in trouble but certainly not doomsday levels honeybees are dealing with their own set of nerves called colony collapse disorder this is a separate thing from the insect pocalypse so we are going to go into too much detail other types of bees wasps and hornets are generally doing alright although the other bee builds in particular are actually being shut out a bit by the invasive honeybees plenty of non arthropod builds will become less viable with fewer insect players around the two main groups affected are the  
* fish and the birds fish have a pretty easy matchup against aquatic insects especially the ones which leave the water once they're in their most powerful final form so without as many of these players around fish we'll need to find another source of easy XP a similar thing can be said about birds many birds rely on flying insect mains as an easy source of XP \- although birds generally have a lot more options besides insects pollinators are some of the most heavily affected so the human playerbase is also potentially in trouble because  
* of their reliance on pollinated crops however many of the humans crops like corn are pollinated by wind so I wouldn't get too worried if your maining human right now anyways overall I don't think the insect apocalypse balance patch is people initially felt insect builds are among the most adaptable and versatile in the game and because of their extremely quick respawn time they were able to come up with counter strategies relatively quickly in addition these nerfs are mostly just due to Resources becoming more limited  
* the climate change balance patch has very little to do with the insect pocalypse and honestly if the human player base really wanted to they're powerful enough to reverse it planting native plants is basically all that needs to be done in order to reverse the effects and like I said insects respond so quickly that that will easily bounce right back if the right conditions exist so there's plenty of reason to have hope in this case by the way I got a lot of the footage in this video from some extremely talented wildlife  
* photographers I really respect the work that they do and I hope to be able to reach the same quality myself someday that's why I've been taking photography classes on Skillshare Skillshare is an online learning community with over 25,000 classes in design business technology and more Premium Membership gives you unlimited access to high-quality classes on must note topics so you can improve your skills unlock new opportunities and do the work you love for example this photography course helped me understand a lot of the basics  
* for shooting outdoors join the millions of students already learning on Skillshare today with a special offer just for my subscribers get two months of Skillshare for free to sign up visit the link in the description and the first 500 visitors will get two months of unlimited access for free back down for the special offer and start learning today anyways thank you all so much for watching and thanks especially my patrons on patreon until next time good luck out there \[Music\]

# 탭 27

* \[Music\] cats and dogs are both some of the most overpowered builds in the entire game but in order to reach that level of success their player bases needed to try out a ton of different strategies both caniforms and feliforms have a powerful solitary tank Buster on their roster they both have midsized scavengers on their roster and perhaps most interestingly both developed slender generalist builds with an emphasis on digging and Brash intimidation on the caniform side we have the mellid builds like weasels ferrets and honey badgers  
* and on the feliform side we have the Mongoose despite being a close relative of cats and sharing a lot of abilities with them they also have a lot of unique traits that set them apart from every other Predator build in the game so are they actually better than their more traditional feline counterparts or are their special abilities just G micke drank today we'll discuss we'll begin as always with a look at the Mongo's base stats starting off with the Mongo's intelligence Mongo's have an intelligence similar to  
* that of a rat which is to say they're quite clever monges are generalists and need to be able to think on their feet in a variety of situations in order to succeed but their best usage of intelligence is in coordinated team tactics team tactics are a rarity in the Fior faction of the malal player base but among the small pool of social feliforms you'd be hardpressed to find a more tactical Bunch the most obvious example is the mircat which is a type of mongoose that specializes specifically in being extra well adapted to work  
* together but most are at the very least gregarious and because Mongo's are much more vulnerable than builds like the lion or wolf they need to work together both in order to succeed in hunting and to watch each other's backs and protect each other their power set is pretty mid for a carnivorous m to be honest unlike most members of the feliform faction mongus do not have retractable claws they use their claws primarily for digging which worked quite well but of course inevitably dulls their claws and makes them less useful  
* for dealing damage as a result Mongo's have to rely solely on their bite force to inflict damage which is unfortunately nothing special still it's enough to get the job done against low HP targets which is really all you need as a midsized social Predator it just means that they can't really threaten an attacking player and need to seed territory whenever pressured now due to the Mongo's slender shape you might assume that similar to honey badgers States and ferrets the Mongoose Sports thick loose skin that allows it to take reduced damage and  
* avoid Critical Hits unfortunately monges do not have this perk and need to play a much less Reckless game than honeybadgers in the face of danger this lack of thick loose skin also makes them less resistant to cold damage forcing them to stick to servers where ice damage isn't a threat one unique defensive tray they do have however is total resistance to Venom their blood neutralizes most toxins rendering them almost completely harmless towards the Mongoose this is the main reason their match up against snakes and scorpions is  
* so oppressive the Mongo's Mobility stat is also fairly mid for a feliform while certainly quite Nimble and agile its actual run speed is below average for a mammal even discounting the cheetah as an outlier most cats have a movement speed that is well above average and are excellent climbers the Mongoose can still move much quicker than any reptile it might encounter but compared to Mamon Predators it might face it is a bit slow its slender body and shorter legs do not offer any benefits for Speed Instead This shape allows is to remain low  
* to the ground taking advantage of cover more easily and also enabling it to access the tight spaces and Burrows that its main threats cannot follow them into other than their short stature mongus don't really have any major stealth-based abilities that they use so their stealth stat is only average as well perception wise the is truly one of the most unique builds in the game if you watched my last video you'll remember that I spoke at length how important the house cat's snake-like vertical slit pupil is for ensuring high  
* accuracy on its attacks which enables the cat to Parry and Counterattack incredibly effectively even if they don't have vertical slit pupils most Predators will at least have circular pupils that will allow for optimal forward binocular vision Mongo's have horror horizontal pupils horizontal pupils do not enhance depth perception and attack accuracy instead the purpose of a horizontal pupil is to broaden the player's field of view making it easier to detect incoming attacks from the side or from behind in addition to this  
* unconventional eye structure is also have an excellent sense of smell and hearing so how well do these stats and abilities synergize with each other honestly it's a pretty mixed bag because the Mongo's stats are generally a bit on the low side they're not able to outrun or repel attackers instead they have to rely on their digging ability for safety this greatly limits the potential territory they can explore as if they get too far from their Burrows it's unlikely they'd be able to escape an ambush it also means that any player  
* with the ability to pursue them into their burrow completely negates their main line of defense the Mongo's eyesight is truly a confounding inclusion in an otherwise fairly standard mesopredator game plan now credit where it's due horizontal pupils do offer some extra benefits that Sy drives well with Mongo's game plan they offer a slight perception bonus in bright sunlight conditions compared to vertical or round pupils because Mongo's are dial in direct sunlight the tan dirt of the African Savannah could impede  
* their Vision if they didn't have a way to mitigate it but ultimately plenty of Savannah builds make do without this so I have to assume the real purpose of this ability is the expanded field of view which primarily AIDS in avoiding ambushes this reflects a higher than normal vulnerability to attacks from predators which I consider the mark of a less than excellent build if I were to stop things here but I do think this does have one more important Synergy for the Mongo's play style the expanded field of view allows the to more  
* easily keep track of where its teammates are improving its ability to coordinate with the movements of other mongooses this is reflected in its exceptional unity and fluidity at which the teams function while plenty of other builds engage in team strategies Mongo's are unique in how tight a formation they're able to hold to this is surprisingly useful for intimidation as when the mongoo band moves in such tight formation they appear as just one Mass which can easily confuse and startle large Predators long enough to make an  
* escape so how does the Mongoose Faire in its most important matchups even deciding which matchups to include was difficult given how widespread the Mongoose player base is but I'll keep things broad enough that it should be applicable to Mongoose Mains everywhere Mongo's famously have an overwhelmingly positive match up against snakes due to their venom immunity this is more important than it first seems obviously it's good to be able to score easy kills and Feed The Troop but snakes are also normally the biggest threat to burrowing  
* builds as they can easily Slither into tight spaces and recab burrowing builds that don't possess Venom immunity like prairie dogs have huge difficulty dealing with snakes so being able to turn this vulnerability into a strength plays hugely in the Mongo's favor against larger Predators Mongo's do struggle they're the main reason they need to remain close enough to their burrow and party members as without them to Aid in escaping attacks they cannot defend themselves so against big cats dogs hyenas and the like the Mongoose  
* player needs to take advantage of all of its outs and work with its teammates to avoid disaster skilled players can hold their own in this match up but mistakes will be punished hard warthogs are one of the most surprising encounters a mongus can have rather than being adversarial warthog and Mongoose players often form temporary alliances the Mongoose team will cleanse the warthog player of all parasite debuffs and in return well the warthog won't really deliberately do anything to help out amongus but simply by tolerating them in  
* close proximity the warthog is protecting them because they're extremely dangerous and normally very territorial Mongo's are much safer when warthogs are nearby and can Venture further from their Burrows so long as they remain within the line of sight of a warthog Birds of Prey are one of the most dangerous matchups for Mongo's because their perception stat tends to be extremely high they will often Target you before you spotted them leaving very little time to prepare defense or Escape however because their power stat tends  
* to be a bit lower often there is a bit of time between the initial strike and the actual kill meaning that if your team can rally quick enough they may actually be able to save you from an attack so once again while technically quite a difficult matchup skilled players with reliable teammates should be able to hold their own drawing comparisons to similarly sized solo builds like the weasel and fet their matchup against an Aven attacker is a lot more hopeless this is essentially the story of the mongus overall it has an unimpressive set of  
* stats and abilities but because the abilities it does have are primarily focused on enabling team strategies a troop of skilled Mongoose Mains can often be far more successful than you would expect from such an underwhelming build overall I'm going to have to give the a mid-tier rating it has no severe flaws holding it back but imagine how overpowered a clan of mongoose players would be if it actually had any busted abilities like with the Mongoose when a group of individuals functions as one unified cohesive team it can look  
* pretty impressive and can be a spectacle to watch however one thing that may be even more fun to watch is when one player on a team is trying to subtly sabotage the group's efforts I was recently on a hilarious game show with this exact premise I know it's weird to think of me as a filthy tryhard human man rather than an omniscient disembodied voice but it's true I do exist in corporeal form the incredibly talented team at wover Productions and jet lag the game invited me to be a part of a game show that they were making  
* what they told me was that a group of including myself were going to be tasked with completing a bunch of wacky challenges and that my job was to play the snitch the sabur who would try and subtly mess up to prevent the group from succeeding what they didn't tell me was that they told everyone else the exact same thing as you can imagine this resulted in one of the funniest most chaotic shows in existence and I seriously could not be more proud to have been a part of it we truly made something awesome and you absolutely  
* have to check it out it's called The Getaway and it's streaming exclusively on nebula the sponsor of this video nebula if you haven't heard is the creator-owned streaming platform with no ads no algorithm no clickbait and phenomenal content home to a huge library of high budget original videos including our show the getaway but also tons of other cool things like my let's play outside series real life Lord's modern conflict Series this awesome deep sea exploration documentary the raising of the Titanic and a fun bushcrafting  
* series called archaeology Quest I also publish all of my videos there early so if you want to watch me try and Bs my way through a bunch of wacky challenges and want to keep up to date on all of my videos and want access a ton of other amazing content on top of all of that you can subscribe to nebula by scanning the QR code on screen or clicking the link in the description if you sign up for an annual membership nebula is only $3 a month truly one of the best deals in streaming so please go check out the getaway only on nebula thanks again to

# 탭 28

* this video was made in partnership with curiosity stream \[Music\] \[Applause\] \[Music\] \[Music\] \[Music\] team strategies are among the most effective things players can do in the game teaming up can enable players to tackle challenges far outside their ability to do so alone and allows players to cover each other's weaknesses in addition to things like pack-hunting there's another type of team strategy worth discussing parenting parenting is  
* a high level strategy that can allow new players to flourish when they'd normally be easy pickings for mid to high level players while team strategies aren't super new they also haven't always been the norm today we're going to discuss how the meta of team strategies developed and it all started during the Permian meta the expansion directly preceding the worst balanced patch of all time the great dying Dimetrodon was one of the top tier builds during that time but what was it about them that gave him such an  
* advantage during the Permian meta Dimetrodon had relatively normal base stats nothing too crazy here even though it's part of the proto mammal guild called synapsids it also shared a lot of traits with the basic dinosaur build it's short legs meant it's mobility stat maxed out fairly low it's power stat was great for the time but not revolutionary and at low stealth due to its huge conspicuous sale and its intelligence and defense were pretty standard for its time the most notable ability in its arsenal was its sale which granted a few  
* perks first it granted huge intimidation bonus because it made the player look like they were in a much higher weight class than they really were this helps prevent attacks from other players but honestly Dimetrodon was the top-tier build at that time and everyone knew it so the intimidation wasn't that useful the sale also prevents the player from being grabbed from behind and flipped upside down which can be extremely important in order to avoid getting a weak point attacked for massive damage the sales most important job was to  
* manage stamina regeneration cold-blooded builds do not regenerate stamina automatically but rather require external heat to recharge a sale allows the user to capture a large amount of solar energy quickly letting the user quickly refresh themselves for the next time that they need a quick burst of speed or strength surprisingly this ability hasn't been used too many times by other bills possibly because it increases the size of your characters hurt box by quite a bit all of this is fine but none of this is exceptional by any means know what made  
* dimension on top-tier was that it was one of the first bills to incorporate parental care into its play style players are at their most vulnerable at the start of the game during the respawn process and before they level up in fact one highly effective strategy to score easy points is just to loot a player's spawn point and interrupt the respawn process before it completes of course the obvious counter strategy to this would be for match level players to guard the spawn point from attacks at least until the respawn is finished surprisingly it took  
* him to a patch one point one point six for players to realize how important and effective this strategy is before that even the top tears mostly left their nests unguarded and they were easy pickings for an experienced player the Metrodome was one of the first to implement the strategy and it did so to great effect many of the other mid and high tier builds during the Permian meta relied heavily on raiding nests and Dimetrodon was one of the few builds that actually made this difficult for them aside from defending his nest from  
* attacks the Metrotown players also perfected the art of making sure new dimension on players spawn in with a full HP bar they do this by incubating the eggs themselves rather than just laying them and hope they don't freeze or overheat which would cause the new players to spawn in with some pretty heavy debuffs if at all but instead they spawn and ready to run which is important because the parental care meta was not very developed at the time once they had hatched the hatchlings would actually agro the other dimension odd it  
* wasn't until far layer that players started also protecting low-level players too but once that strategy started getting some traction it wasn't long before just about every building the game adopted it and started incorporating it into their play styles this strategy is one of the main reasons that mammals became the dominant guild so quickly after the dinosaur extinction balance patch they invest such a large amount of time into new players that by the time they're done with their tutorial levels they're already powerful  
* enough to hold their own in combat but it's not just mammals that now invest time into their offspring during the Mesozoic expansion dinosaurs also picked up this technique the large predatory dinosaur bills were nearly unstoppable one on one so low level herbivore Mane's didn't stand a chance against them however herbivore players quickly discovered that team defense strategies were quite effective at keeping their low level players alive even though most dinosaurs eventually got banned the techniques that they developed persisted  
* in the avian faction which has to carefully tend to their new players until they level up enough to unlock the flight ability but while birds do it well mammals are the pioneers of the teamwork and parenting strategies the synapsid guild which eventually became the mammal guild continued to perfect their techniques all throughout the Mesozoic expansion and through the Cenozoic \- and in particular were the first skilled to unlock a special ability called live birth which lets players bring their respawn points with them instead of  
* having to build a nest to hide egg spawn points in this is a huge advantage because now to spawn new players in the player only needs to stay alive rather than protect an objective for months and at this point the mammal player base is known for putting an absolutely massive amount of effort into raising up new mammal players they have the longest respawn times which actually lets player spawn and having already leveled up a few times they also produce a substance that causes new players to level up quickly which gives them a huge  
* advantage over builds like arthropods reptiles and amphibians which have to find sources of XP on their own right from the start this isn't to say that none of those classes have adopted team strategies or parenting though plenty of reptiles and even arthropods engage in at least some form of defending new players or team attacks but nothing compares to the parenting power of the mammal guild mammals also get a rage power of when they defend their young a buff so powerful that for even the most Opie builds in the game challenging and  
* raged mother player often means game over so the next time you see a mammal player bodying its competition you'll at least understand the abilities that grant them such a competitive edge in their early game and you'll know precisely which players pioneered these techniques and strategies to the synapses if you'd like to learn more about the evolution of parental care in synapsids there's an excellent documentary called leaps in evolution that I highly recommend you check out it features some incredible visuals of synapses and  
* dinosaurs in their fight to survive some of the best I've ever seen in fact it's available for streaming right now on curiosity scene the best place on the internet for high-quality nonfiction content it was created by the man behind the Discovery Channel and features some of my all-time favorite paleo documentaries such as Walking with Dinosaurs out of the cradle and of course leaps in evolution the subscription is $2.
