# 탭 29

* this video was made in partnership with curiosity stream despite starting out as a small niche rogue built from within the dinosaur faction the avian playerbase has diversified to an incredible degree given that dinosaurs had naturally high power stats it's no surprise that some birds ended up opting for a DPS strategy but what is surprising is how many bird players SPECT in too high intelligence the two leaders in this department are the corvids and the parrots and while roughly equivalent in terms of their  
* base intelligence stat parrots are far better suited to the intelligence playstyle for several reasons the parrot has a lot going for it with several powerful abilities complementing its great base stats so in this video I'll be going over all of the parents signature abilities in order to conclude where their overall places in the meta let's begin with the parrot stealth most parent players deliberately chose an abysmal stealth level instead opting for vibrant colors that make avoiding detection near impossible lacking  
* camouflage may seem risky for a build with less than impressive defensive stats and it's certainly not without its challenges but what this does accomplish is granting the parrot opponents to all intimidation attempts as the vibrant colors can surprise and convey whose opponents this strategy is normally only used in builds that are dependent on poison for defense so it's a bit unorthodox for a non-toxic build like the parrot to adopt the strategy the reason this intimidation strategy was so effective was that it had another  
* complimentary ability the searing streak one of the loudest most powerful sonic type attack with roughly the same volume as a gunshot this can easily break the resolve of a nearby player causing it to flee from battle for defense parrots don't really have much to talk about here their feathers do offer some protection from crush type damage but this is pretty minimal the fact that parents have hollow bones means that crush type damage is actually super effective against them anyway for mobility I mean there's the obvious  
* parrots are solid fliers now of course they aren't as agile as say gauze Hawks or swallows nor are they able to traverse entire oceans without resting like the albatross but flight is self life and because of this they're able to evade just about any ground-based attack so why is the parents mobility so high well this is because there are also excellent climbers flight is usually difficult in dense forests and jungles as there's a lot of obstacles that a flying player needs to dodge constantly but because of the parrots zygodactylous  
* climbing through a dense jungle canopy is no problem as they can grip onto most surfaces with ease for their vitality stat there's not much worth discussing except to mention that parrots have extremely long lifespans four-power parrot ranks surprisingly high while not quite as devastating as the Eagles dagger-like talents or a Falcons razor-sharp beak parrots do still have a seriously powerful bite most bird builds have a beak optimized to deal stab type damage with the move Peck replacing unique bite in their moveset pecking is  
* faster has less n leg and is a better move in most cases the beak of a parrot in contrast deals Crush type damage while not as useful in PvP this gives them the ability to crunch through tough items like for example tree nuts which are worth lots of experience if you can crack them with that being said despite its low range a parent player can still deal serious damage with their bite if they can land a hit this is all well and good but let's be real nobody is maining parrot for its damage or arboreal movement bonuses the main  
* draw of this build is its intelligence so how good is the parrot in the role of an intelligence build although intelligence is arguably the most powerful stat in the game hi intelligence alone is not a top-tier make a good build needs other useful features to make use of intelligence the parrot build has this in spades what's so impressive about the parrot is that all of its abilities have secondary effects that synergize extremely well with a high intelligence statin for example in addition to being able to  
* deal some pretty serious sonic damage the parrots syrinx is also a highly versatile communication ability being able to easily converse with other parrots over short and medium distances this is invaluable for facilitating team strategies like for example requesting reinforcements to help drive away enemy player with multiple simultaneous sonic attacks or forewarning allies of an aggressive enemy player parrots are extremely social and because of their highly effective communication techniques they can form some of the  
* tightest bonds of any faction in fact because of how versatile their syrinx is they can even learn to communicate with other non parrot players an ability few have unlocked but it doesn't stop there the parrots flexible beak and a zygodactylous 'full for climbing both have the secondary function of allowing the user to grab items or other players this is actually one of the main reasons I consider parrots to be superior to corvatz in terms of their ability to function as an intelligence but as crows have a much harder time gripping things  
* than parrots do the reason this matters is that being able to pick up and manipulate objects in the environment is the main requirement for the top tier intelligence mobility tool use while there are plenty of builds with an intelligence that high enough to allow them to equip tools many of them lack the actual ability to do so for example pigs and dolphins parrots are one of the few builds with great general dexterity and lastly I want to bring up the parrots vitality again as a long lifespan is a trait that is most  
* beneficial to intelligence builds parrots actually have one of the longest lifespans in the entire game rivaling the elephant and trailing only humans Greenland sharks and builds that have the trait negligible senescence like jellyfish and clams this means that parrot players have a long time to learn skills both through independent practice and through the teaching of higher level players this again sets them apart from corvids who unfortunately only have a fraction of a lifespan the parrot build has been on the decline  
* lately as jungle servers get taken down and converted to farms and as human players capture them in order to sell them to other human players who want to add them to their party however as with most top \- your intelligence builds parrots are highly adaptable and because of their unique skillset many of them were able to escape captivity and establish entirely new player bases in several city servers I consider this to be the hallmark of a top-tier build to be best SPECT for gameplay in one environment but able to cope with shifts  
* in the meta and survive in an extremely different environment to get a more in-depth look at the story of how the parrot faction has adapted to spread across a range of different city servers I recommend the documentary planet parrot a lot of the beautiful parrot footage featured in this video is from that documentary and I consider it to be extremely well made you can watch planet parrot as well as thousands of other excellent titles on curiosity stream the premier sponsor of tears in curiosity stream is a subscription streaming service that  
* you've probably heard me sing the praises up for a while seeing as they've been this channels sole sponsor for the entirety of 2020 so far and have continued to do so despite certain game updates making gameplay rather difficult for human mates they've also partnered with nebula another subscription streaming service that's actually run by youtubers including myself I have an original video on nebula that I'd love for you to check out and as long as you use the link in the description to subscribe to curiosity stream you'll also get access  
* to nebula as a free bonus considering the membership is only three dollars a month this is one of the best ways to support creators like me so head to curiosity Stream calm slash tears you to get started check out planet parrot miner Bueller originals and all the other awesome titles features on there last but not least huge thank you to our patrons on patreon and thank you for watching until ne

# 탭 30

* \[Music\] foreign \[Music\] is one of the most formidable builds in the game though they may not be the largest fastest or smartest builds in the current meta they're undoubtedly top five when it comes to raw power and defense their most iconic feature their massive fearsome horn has a wide variety of uses both in and out of combat this combined with the Rhino's other special abilities make it seem like an overpowered top tier build but rhinos have some less obvious weaknesses that might change the equation a bit in  
* today's video we'll go over all the pros and cons to determine so first let's take an in-depth look at the rhinoceros's unique traits and special abilities starting of course with its horn the rhino's horn is one of the most powerful weapons not just in the current meta but in the entire history of the game while not all of the variants of the Rhino build invest heavily into the horn skill tree some rhinos particularly the White Rhino have horns which can get up to five feet long with the full force of a charging  
* Behemoth behind it this horn can easily one hit any player unfortunate enough to get caught at the other end this length gives Rhino players a massive forward threat range that other players can often underestimate especially when factoring in the Rhinos surprisingly high speed stat with a top speed of roughly 30 miles per hour they often catch careless players off guard with how quickly that giant horn can get within sparing distance now oftentimes one of the main weaknesses of this sort of playstyle I.E the kind of build with  
* a large powerful skull mounted weapon is the that if an opponent is able to get inside of your optimal threat range either by dodging your first attack and punishing the end lag or by approaching from a difficult to defend angle you may have little left in terms of defenses and might just be out of options to fight off the attack this is not the case with the Rhino because the Rhino has the best defense stats of any build in his weight class it has extremely thick skin which is packed full of fibrous material making it highly  
* resistant to piercing damage in addition to allowing rhinos to spar with each other without risking an instant game over this also allows rhinos to shrug off attacks from literally any predator build from the crushing bite of a hyena to the Razer blade-like swipe of a tiger's claws rhinos aren't completely Invincible they're not bulletproof and can't tank hits forever but they've got a lot more room for mistakes than most builds with all that being said the rhinoceros does have plenty of weaknesses the first of which is their  
* extremely poor eyesight rhinos have eyes on the side of their head which is meant to give them a wide field of view at the cost of binocular vision aka the ability to judge the distance to another player or object in 3D space however a rhino's vision is so poor that it can only detect basic shapes past roughly 10 yards meaning they cannot detect other players if they remain motionless and what you get is a rhino which can see all of the movement around itself but is unable to quickly gauge whether or not that movement represents a threat and so  
* you'll commonly see Rhino Mains getting stressed out and startled by the simplest of things and also rhinos which attack anything that moves on site this of course is highly exploitable any stealth-based build will have an easy time concealing its presence from Rhino until it wants to make a move but this vulnerability is also easily exploited by intelligence-based builds such as the human or the elephant by tactically using items or team strategies High Intelligence builds can easily distract or kite a rhinoceros into doing exactly  
* what they wanted whether it be to force to retreat from a fight that it could have won or bait the rhinoceros into a trap this lack of ability to discern friend from foe means the Rhino's potential allies may not be willing to allow Rhino to join their party since the Rhino may accidentally Target a party member with an attack if it feels threatened thankfully there is one build which does make for a willing and effective team combo with the Rhino and that is the ox pecker oxpecker Birds provide their allies with a low-grade  
* parasite resistance buff which is an important perk given how op the tick build is in the current meta but more importantly for the Rhino the oxpecker can essentially function as the Allies third eye watching their party members back and alerting them of any approaching enemies and because the ox peckers are so small and agile they're not really at risk of being attacked by an overly aggressive Rhino now although rhinos can dish out some of the highest damage in the entire game to anything unlucky enough to be caught in front of  
* their giant horns another major weakness of their players is their inability to threaten players approaching from behind them most herbivore builds even those with equally impressive horns make sure they spec into a powerful kick to ensure that they don't just automatically lose to any aggressor that manages to get behind them a rhino's legs are not tall enough nor fast enough to threaten a powerful kick and so when a rhino gets surrounded its only option is to tank quite a few hits now of course their thick armor does immensely help in this  
* situation but no armor in this game is completely impervious and give it enough time a persistent Predator can still deal damage even to a heavily armored rhinoceros this weakness is made worse by the fact that the Rhino like all mammals has a weak point on its hindquarters called the perennium taking damage to this area can inflict a serious blood loss debuff which can be enough to bring down even the Rhino normally it's the job of a player's teammates to guard their backside like this but given the Rhino's propensity  
* for a friendly fire they can't always count on having allies that are willing to help unfortunately as helpful as the oxpacker birds are they aren't likely to try and challenge a lion in the middle of an ambush so there's definitely quite a few downsides that come with playing this Powerhouse of a build there's also some variation within the Rhino faction allowing for players to optimize for their desired play style if you just want to max out on your attack damage you'll want to play as the White Rhino which sports the most  
* powerful horn and largest size if you care more about mobility and armor you can opt for the Indian rhino which is smaller and only has a single horn but has better Mobility both on land and in the water and is also the more heavily armored of the two next let's talk about individual matchups first big cats rhinos have essentially nothing to fear from the felid build sharp as their claws might be they simply aren't powerful enough to penetrate a rhino's armor big cats kill with a strangling bite to the throat and there's basically  
* zero chance of this attack strategy working against a rhino especially seeing as any attempt at this would put them right in range of the Rhino's primary attack next elephants rhinos are one of the few builds which actually can genuinely threaten serious damage against an elephant elephants don't have any or near as much armor as a rhino so even in direct 1v1 the rhino's horn is likely to out damage the elephant's tusks if the Rhino can to get within range unfortunately the elephant has two major advantages over the Rhino the  
* first is its range the elephant's tusks outrange the rhino's horn so even though the elephant's DPS is lower than the Rhinos the elephant's tusks can push the Rhino out of range and prevent it from being able to put its horn to work the second Advantage is its intelligence elephants are especially Adept at exploiting the Rhino's lower intelligence and poor eyesight by throwing objects towards a rhino elephants can easily induce the frightened status condition in a rhino causing the Rhino to Forfeit a fight it  
* potentially could have stood a solid chance in had it held its ground to be fair it's not the worst indictment to have a losing matchup against an elephant hardly anything has a favorable matchup against them but it's unfortunate that if the Rhino didn't have these weaknesses it actually would have a solid matchup against the elephant despite the size difference next the hippopotamus hippos and rhinos are very similar in terms of their base stats they're very similar in size roughly equivalent in their base movement speed and each you can dish out  
* incredible damage the hippo has higher base HP but significantly less armor this is by far the most even matchup on this list and a confrontation can easily go either way the Rhino's susceptibility to intimidation does cause it to sometimes back down from fights that probably could win but there are also plenty of cases of overly aggressive hippos getting hard punished for approaching too carelessly hyenas as mentioned before although the Rhino does have excellent armor the only Predator that even realistically has a shot at  
* inflicting damage to them would be the hyena Which packs one of the strongest bite forces anything a rhino could ever encounter second only to the crocodile it's still extremely rare for a pack of hyenas to bring down a rhino but by persistently chipping away at the Rhino's rear weak point it can happen the Rhino has a solid match-up spread but despite all of their incredible advantages still end up taking a lot more L's than they should due to their heavily exploitable weaknesses although they're able to tank a lot of hits as an  
* adult their low intelligence stat allows enemy players to kite protective parents away from the low-level rhinos they were guarding whether that be through distraction intimidation or team tactics and with a respawn time of almost a year and a half every defeat hits the Rhino player base exceptionally hard the Rhino build is quite powerful but I actually think it's a bit over invested in his combat stats at the cost of its own efficiency this is likely because the Rhino build was originally meant for much colder climates where larger size  
* was also simply an efficient design choice to provide the player with a cold resistance buff during the Ice Age the ancient variants of the Rhino such as the woolly rhinoceros absolutely needed this extra bulk in order to fight off powerful threats like the short-faced bear and Cave hyena but with the largest threat to rhinos in the modern day being the tiger rhinos don't really need this much physical force to be competitively viable there also used to be a lot more variety to the rhinoceros player base paracer ethereum was the largest  
* terrestrial mammal build ever seen in the game and essentially functioned as a Malian sauropod and yet for some reason the tall Grazer Niche is dominated by the giraffe instead of a variant of the rhinoceros even though a paracer ethereum could easily win a 1v1 against giraffe this may be a bit controversial but taking into account their many weaknesses and seeing how out of place they are in the current meta compared to the Ice Age I'm going to place the rhinoceros into deter low intelligence in particular leaves the Rhino helpless  
* in the face of certain strategies however intelligence is something that can be leveled up with the right sort of training in today's meta abilities like coding math and data science are extremely useful they are tough to learn unless you got a free and easy course designed to intuitively guide you through difficult Concepts that's precisely where today's sponsor brilliant comes in brilliant is an online learning platform which uses fun interactive courses to help you level up those science and engineering skills I

# 탭 31

* scorpions are one of the most intimidating builds in the game they're also one of the oldest and have been in the game since before the devs even added the terrestrial server dlc their combination of powerful claws and deadly stinger create an imposing threat to anyone looking to challenge them in combat but has their ancient strategy held up in the face of the immense power creep the game has seen let's take a closer look at the stats and abilities scorpions employ and ultimately decide where they fall on the tier list this video was made in  
* partnership with curiosity stream first they're stats the scorpion's stats are fairly decent across the board nothing completely overtuned but not really lacking in any areas other than mobility and intelligence their power is obviously quite strong as the scorpion sports multiple types of damage dealing equipment their claws act as potent grappling weapons which can also deal minor crushing and slicing damage their venom sting is their ace move though being able to one shot most players if the move connects  
* big if there but we'll come back to that their defenses are quite sturdy though they sport the fairly standard kite and armor found in most arthropod builds this is all they really need to be able to deflect most attacks from players within their own weight class giving it ample time to counter-attack with its piercing stinger it gets easily crushed by larger foes however this armor is also crucial for avoiding passive environmental damage from things like sandstorms which are common in the biomes scorpion mains favor  
* despite the fact that humans have a way to completely negate the scorpion's camouflage against most opponents the scorpion's stealth is quite effective in addition to camouflage the scorpion's flat shape allows it to easily wedge itself into more defensible positions and their digging ability enables them to take cover in a pinch which is good because stealth is basically their only option against more powerful players they have very little defense against attacks from larger builds you'd think their potent venom would make for an  
* effective deterrent against attacks but as we'll see this defense falls apart rather quickly when it's truly tested their intelligence being below average is no surprise intelligence builds weren't viable at all when scorpions were first added to the game and while it's by far the most power crept stat in the game now back during the ordovician expansion intelligence as a strategy was largely ignored and most players didn't level it beyond the point of granting basic sensory capabilities so with all that said scorpions are a  
* solid build with a straightforward strategy and a respectable history however as alluded to earlier power creep has massively undercut the viability of scorpion builds a good player will have very little difficulty playing around the scorpion's strengths and exploiting its major weaknesses so let's talk about those weaknesses the straightforward nature of the scorpion strategy is great for new players to be able to learn quickly the scorpion's core strategy is to grapple an opponent immobilizing them so that the scorpion's sting attack can connect  
* without issue the problem is if they miss their grapple attempt things can get dicey pretty quickly without claws holding the target in place the venom sting attack has a pretty terrible accuracy rating it's easily dodged blocked or avoided worse still this stinger is not a disjointed hitbox it can be damaged just the same as the rest of the scorpion's body in fact when dealing with a scorpion this is the optimal strategy scorpion claws are fairly weak compared to other clawed arthropods hermit crabs and lobsters can both deal  
* far more damage via their vice grip attacks scorpions rarely deal lethal damage in such a way so if an opponent attacks and disables their stinger it's often game over for the scorpion scorpions also have very little counter play when faced with multiple opponents with no multi-target or aoe attacks and no means of quick escape they're quickly overwhelmed from a multi-directional onslaught their grab attacks are also not as effective as they need to be at times because they mostly rely on the pointed ridges of their claws to  
* maintain grip armored targets that can resist being punctured by these spines can often slip right out of their grip with ease not that the scorpion had much hope of landing a sting against a target with high puncture resistance anyway now let's talk about match-ups first the bad of which there are many the scorpion's worst match-ups are going to be builds that spec into puncture resistant armor or venom resistance several mammal builds like the shrew and mongoose opt for venom resistance solely for the purpose of subverting the  
* scorpion's defenses which makes this a borderline unwinnable matchup the round shape of beetle armor is perfect for deflecting sting attacks and extremely difficult to secure via grappling beetles tend to also be fairly lacking in their offensive stats so it is rare to actually be defeated by one but as a hunting target they present quite a challenge ants obviously must be avoided at all costs as scorpions have no outs against a swarming attack birds have high accuracy on their packing attacks and are often quite good  
* at targeting their stingers their quick movement presents very little opportunity for counter-attack making this a tough matchup certainly not impossible though as one hit from the stinger is likely enough to ko any bird and their feathers provide zero puncture resistance reptiles are kind of a toss-up an attack from a lizard or snake is usually powerful enough to deal heavy damage through the scorpion's armor however these attacks usually present ample opportunity for a stinging counter attack too if it connects it may one  
* shot the reptile but not before it has already dealt potentially lethal damage anyway scale armor is also somewhat puncture resistant so this combined with the sting's low accuracy means these fights tend to favor the reptile for good matchups scorpions are actually quite effective against spiders they lack strong armor and need to get in close to deliver their own lethal strike presenting lots of opportunity for counter-attacks their webbing is of course a threat but it's difficult for a spider to wet down all three weapons especially since the  
* claws can slice through silk scorpions are also fairly decent at defending themselves from mammals like cats and dogs as their claws cannot do much damage against the scorpion's armor but the scorpion can easily counter attack for lethal if the mammal gets too careless there are a few abilities i think could turn scorpions into a top tier threat a more versatile grappling option would be a solid start their claws struggle to secure opponents with puncture resistance or round shape both of which are fairly abundant in the current meta  
* but there are plenty of other grappling techniques they could spec into instead of pincher claws the velvet worms glue spray attack could work well to immobilize prey though this might not be super effective when trying to pin a player to a sandy surface they could spec into the sort of tether grab attack like the chameleon uses though this requires a level of precision and accuracy that i don't think the scorpion's current perception perks can quite support scorpions could really benefit from catching up to the  
* other arthropods who run the compound eyes perk as this would really help alleviate their accuracy problem one strat i think would work really well would be to min max for the burrowing play style a bit more deliberately the antlions play style of building a trap could be really effective and the scorpion is already fairly decent at digging holes the added protection of staying out of sight would help against some of their worst matchups too as they are now though to be honest i do think scorpions have been powercraft  
* pretty hard and that their glory days of being a top meta threat are long gone their powerful grab sting death combo is still quite powerful but their strategy is too easily subverted to make a dent in high level play i place them firmly in d tier on the tier list scorpions are a fascinating build that i do hope eventually get buffed if you'd like to learn more about scorpions there's an excellent documentary i can recommend called animal armor it's part of a series called nature's fight club and this episode centers on animals  
* which prioritize their defensive abilities it has a substantial section all about scorpions but also features incredible builds like the pangolin and porcupine you can check it out on curiositystream the sponsor of today's video curiosity stream is a subscription streaming service where you can watch thousands of high quality documentaries and non-fiction titles including a bunch of my personal favorites like leaps in evolution and out of the cradle in addition every new curiosity stream member also gets access to nebula a  
* streaming service made by and for creators like myself i've made a few exclusive videos on there but i also recommend some of the originals made by fellow creators such as the modern conflicts series made by real life lore it's especially relevant in today's meta to get access to both of these awesome services for only 299 a month head to curiositystream.  
* com tierzoo or click the link in the description thank you so much for watching thanks especially to my patrons on patreon and i'll see you all in the next video \[Music\] you

# 탭 32

* since this has been pretty much my most requested meta-analysis from the start I feel I owe it to you guys to tackle this subject as a thank you for reaching almost ten thousand subscribers in just over a month with that said you guys might not like what I have to say about it water bears judging by how many comments you all made praising the water bear it's obvious none of you have actually ever tried to play that build before let me walk you through the character creation process for water bears with the hopes that you realize  
* that they make basically all of the wrong choices hey looks like you've chosen kingdom Animalia good choice they've got the most interesting gameplay in my opinion okay so from here what are you feeling bilateral symmetry good good choice brains are fun okay so are you feeling endo or exoskeleton oh okay I'm an endo guy myself but I see the value in having armor oh you don't want armor you sure you're so close you've almost backed into the arthropod guild that's got the s tears like spiders and insects well alright so  
* where are we going with this I mean without armor you'll probably need some good evasion abilities or really solid offense right no none of that geez you're gonna be a sitting duck well I guess since you didn't go for any of those you've got a lot of evolution points left what are you going for maybe some poison or maybe some super good camouflage what heat resistance okay unorthodox but sure wait you want ice resistance too why would you want both well okay there you go what else radiation resistance okay exactly how often do you think that's  
* going to come in handy supernova resistance gamma-ray burst resistance asteroid impact resistance exactly what are you expecting to encounter geez your lifespan is only a year oh you'd like to be able to go dormant for half a century sure why not you'll still be a Sigma the second you wake up so do you plan on banning together and fighting as some sort of an army oh you're you're gonna kill each other alright then okay so I think you get the picture the point is Water Bearers are basically resistant to everything except for the  
* things that would actually kill them they're kind of like Doomsday Preppers actually and as a result they get killed by the millions by crabs shrimps worms snails slugs mites spiders scorpions insects and other water bears but hey if it ever runs into an enemy whose only method of attack is a laser cannon they'll be all right it's also important to point out that water bears do not have the extrema file attribute they can persist in ridiculously harsh environments but they can't live in them you get launched into  
* space you're still going to die it's just gonna take you a really long time you want to play a good survivalist class I suggest either the cockroach rat or if you're feeling really extreme you could try it in Archaean one last thing about water bears one of the data miners was analyzing water bears and when you look through the code he found a lot of it was pretty similar to a bacterial code so instead of being like huh how did this bacterial DNA get in my sample he was like wow looks like water bears can absorb DNA and incorporate it into  
* their own genome despite the fact that it would be incompatible yeah that makes sense it's time to publish that and everyone just kind of went along with it I can't tell you how many game guides say something like most alien like genome of any organism or some other nonsense like really it took other data miners a whole year before they realized hey what's more likely an animal that can steal genetic code or that we messed up and even though they did publish an updated study it was too late the myth had become ingrained in players minds so  
* yeah don't buy into the hype water bears are one of the weakest classes you could possibly play thanks again for all the support over this first month everyone this channel has gone further than I ever imagined and most of you seem to want it to go even further

# 탭 33

* Okay, so there's a lot of people out there who think they've got the zoology tier list down, think they know which build types are the best, and which ones aren't worth the time. Well, I'm here to tell you how wrong you are. There are a lot of hidden high tiers and even top tiers found in some unlikely places.  
* In this video, I'll talk about one of them. Before I get into it, let me give some quick background on one aspect of the meta. So in the current meta, it's a pretty commonly held belief that of all the builds possessing the brain attribute, amphibians rank the lowest sitting comfortably in F tier. And this is not all that unwarranted.  
* Amphibians are extremely sensitive to shifts in the overworld. Even the most minus tweaks such as a change in acidity or water temperature can drastically reduce their viability. And speaking of water, even though that don't have gills, many amphibians are almost totally bound to bodies of water and die if they're away from moisture for any extended period of time.  
* And although amphibians do possess some rare high tier traits like cling pads, saltatorial legs, and webbed feet, this doesn't make up for the downsides like extreme vulnerability to the elements, some of the lowest defensive base stats possible, lack of mobility, lack of strength, and an exceptionally weak start.  
* With that said, there is one amphibian who defies all norms and is undoubtedly near the top of the tier list. And no, before you click away, this is not another video trying to argue that the poison dart frog is top tier. Look. You guys put way too many evolution points into toxins and aesthetics. Yes, you've got the poison with the highest DPS possible as well as very unique coloration, but you should have stopped specking those after you noticed a diminish in returns.  
* If you'd done that, you'd be truly high tier like the subject of this video: the cane toad. The cane toad is the highest-ranking amphibian for the following reasons. Number one: all of the main negatives of this class are kept to a minimum. A respectable amount of evolution points allocated to thicker, leathery skin boosts both physical defense and resistance to the elements.  
* And when combined with a larger size, most lightweight and mobility based builds simply cannot take it down. Now obviously larger size is seen as a risk in the meta due to a higher food cost. But the cane toad build allocates a huge amount of points into hunting and digestion with a specialty in gobbling.  
* Seriously, the mouth of a cane toad is essentially a trash compactor. In addition, an extremely fast growth rate keeps the starting stage vulnerability to a minimum. Finally, the cane toad avoids making the same mistake as the poison dart frog. Investing only a moderate amount of evolution points into toxins grants the cane toad a toxin strong enough to kill a cat and make a dog sick.  
* Although it's nowhere near the elephant herd slaying potential of the poison dart frog, it pretty much gets the job done just as well. And by spending nearly zero points in aesthetics, the cane toad has saved enough to adapt to almost any environment. In fact, they're so dominant in the Australian server that they've caused many low tiers to quit entirely.  
* So there you have it. Amphibians aren't all bottom tier. If you have a build that you'd like a biologist to take on, let me know in the comments. Thanks\!

# 탭 34

* \[Music\] This episode was sponsored by War Thunder. Arthropods have been one of the most dominant factions in the game since pretty much the very beginning. The safety provided by their tough exoskeleletal armor gave the Arthropod player base a lot of room to experiment with new builds. One of the most successful examples is the spider, a unique arthropod build with a whole arsenal of powerful abilities.  
* But just how broken are spiders? Today, we're discussing their stats, their special abilities, and their matchups to demonstrate just how overpowered they truly are. But before we talk about all of their busted attributes, first we have to talk about the least overpowered thing about them, their base stats. While spiders are some of the most effective predators the game has ever known, it's not because their raw stats are anything special.  
* In fact, if anything, their stats make them look pretty pathetic at first glance. There's a common misconception that spiders are like absurdly strong for their size. And although they can indeed lift things 10 times their own body weight, that's honestly not even part for the course when it comes to their weight class.  
* The real kings of the strength category builds like the ant and beetle are several times stronger. In fact, much of the spider's skill set is tailored around allowing spiders to take down large prey without needing to be strong enough to physically restrain it themselves. Defensively, things are even worse.  
* Spiders have some of the worst armor of any arthropod. Despite being more closely related to scorpions, their exoskeleton is more akin to that of a soft, plump caterpillar than anything else. Their weak point is completely unarmored and difficult for the spider to guard against. Any direct attack from a predator can easily one-shot them.  
* Their stealth is their only good stat with the rest of them being mid at best. Of course, that is on average. Some spider builds do specialize in mobility or raw strength, which I talked more about in my spider tier list video. Anyway, the point is what makes spiders broken is not their stats, but their abilities.  
* Spiders have access to a vast array of powerful perks that dramatically elevate their survival and combat capabilities. Real quick, let's go over some of the minor ones first. The spider's appendages may seem pretty unimpressive. And sure, compared to a scorpion's claws or a locust's legs, they're nothing special, but a few things.  
* They do have small claws at the end, which grant spiders excellent climbing mobility. And similar to a lizard's tail, a spider's leg can snap off if hit by a grappling attack in order to escape a combo. Unlike the lizard though, losing only 12% of your legs is a lot less of a sacrifice than losing your only weapon. Many players don't even have the nerve to mess with spiders in the first place, though, because of their next ability, venom.  
* With a few exceptions, every spider relies at least partially on venom to bolster their combat strength. This gives their attacks bonus damage, making it a useful deterrent on defense. But more importantly, their venom inflicts the paralysis status effect, immobilizing their prey so that it can be more easily carried and consumed.  
* Because of how low the spider's base power stat is, this venom often needs to be extremely potent. Able to immediately incapacitate its target, giving it no chance to counterattack or escape. Okay, but enough beating around the bush. All of those abilities pale in comparison to the spider's signature special ability, Spinetses.  
* On this channel, there are a handful of abilities that I bring up over and over as being metadingly broken. tool use, flight, you sociality, echolocation, color change, these all can make or break a character's placement on the tier list. I think silk spinning belongs in this same category. This ability is how spiders broke the game.  
* It is one of the most versatile abilities of all with applications spanning nearly every aspect of survival, from mobility to offense to defense to tons of niche utility use cases. Let's break it down. The most well-known use is, of course, the standard web trap. A spider player will construct a sticky net-shaped trap and simply wait for an unsuspecting player to accidentally fly in.  
* Contact with the web immediately inflicts the restrained status condition, which is pretty much instant GG's versus a build that can one-shot you if it lands a hit. But trapping prey really only scratches the surface when it comes to the utility of silk and spinterettes. I'd argue the ability to spin webs bucks literally every aspect of spider gameplay and completely negates all the shortcomings of their stats.  
* In general, spiders are not all that nimble. Their large abdomen are fairly cumbersome, and without wings, they're far below average as far as arthropods go, but the amount of additional movement options web spinning gives you is insane. Silk can be used to cross massive gaps with ease. It can be used to completely negate falling damage.  
* It can be used to quickly ascend from a low position, and in some cases, it can even be used to fly, albeit without much directional control, but also without the need for cumbersome wings. Next, let's talk about stealth. While many spiders do have decent camouflage, webs complement their ability to hide in a few ways. The first is just that it enables them to hide in plain sight by positioning themselves away from places that a player would normally check.  
* It's very hard for eyes to focus on something unless they already know the general area to look. So, just by nature of sitting in an unorthodox spot, many players will completely miss you. But if a player does detect a spider's presence, there is an advanced technique they can employ to give themselves a better chance to escape.  
* By mashing back and forth on your controller, you can cause the web to vibrate, temporarily granting you the blurred buff, boosting your evasion and making it much more likely that incoming attacks will miss. Webs may be the most useful for boosting the spider's offense. We already talked about the web trap, but there are many more ways a spider can use its webs to increase its odds of a successful hunt.  
* Web traps are most useful against flying builds, but webs can also be cast out as a net to target players scurrying along the ground more effectively, essentially bringing the web to them rather than waiting and hoping they wander in. Or if you want to get even more extreme, silk can be fired as a sticky projectile to pin down opponents at a safe distance.  
* But you don't need to get anywhere near that fancy with it. Even just the basic concept of using a strand of silk to tether yourself to a surface opens up so many attack options in combat. Let me give you an example. Pouncing strikes are one of the riskiest attacking moves a player can employ in combat.  
* They're excellent for closing distance on a target and dealing damage simultaneously, but if you miss, the momentum from the jump can result in your character ending up in a dangerous situation. Pounce is usually seen as the signature move of the cat faction. And in those examples, the worst case scenario is usually just putting yourself in range of a counterattack.  
* But for builds in lower weight classes, and especially aroreal ones, whiffing a pounce attack can literally send you falling to your doom. For spiders, this risk is completely negated with a single silk tether, meaning jumping spiders can get super greedy with their attacks without needing to worry about poor positioning.  
* Silk can even be used to approach from unconventional angles that players aren't used to covering. Great for allowing jumping spiders to attack other spiders weak points. It really is just an incredibly broken ability, especially for the jumping spider, which can combo silk production with its excellent burst mobility. We're not done, though, because silk production can also be used defensively, mainly in the form of base construction.  
* The standard web trap is technically a form of this, though it's probably the least defenseoriented and frankly leaves you quite open to an attack. But skilled players can construct webs in a far more defensive manner if they choose. Styles like the tent web provide much more cover at the cost of visibility.  
* But if you want to really max out your defenses, the trapdoor spider strategy can't be beat. Rather than using a sticky web to trap its opponents, it instead digs a burrow and uses silk to reinforce the walls and help control the temperature inside while also laying out a few trip wires on the outside to allow the spider to detect when other players are in range of an ambush.  
* It is admittedly a bit less precise than more active hunting types are, though. Now, if you really want to go crazy with it, most of the spider's usual enemies aren't going to look for you underwater. So, if you've got the guts to dive down, you can construct a bell web underwater and use that as your home base while you hunt for fish and tadpoles.  
* It's a high-risisk, high reward play style, though, given how many dangerous builds lurk below the surface. Overall, spiders have an incredible array of traits, and with smart play, they can subvert just about all of their weaknesses. However, there are still some vulnerabilities that skilled players can exploit.  
* So, let's talk about their matchups. The spider's best matchups are, of course, flying insects. Their wings get easily tangled in the web and can get torn up as they try to escape. Getting trapped is almost certainly GG for them. Reptiles may seem like they should have a solid match up against spiders. And of course, when there are massive size discrepancies, it can be pretty unfair, but for less tanky reptiles, spiders are a huge threat.  
* Scale armor is great for blocking slashing damage, but piercing fangs can puncture right through their defenses and inflict devastating venomous bites. This matchup also really helps it sink in just how strong spider silk is, as even medium-sized reptiles can't easily shake it off. Frogs, and in particular, tree frogs are an especially explosive matchup.  
* Because both frogs and spiders have incredibly low defenses, one wrong move is all it takes. Frogs can easily one-shot spiders, and because of their excellent burst mobility, it's difficult for spiders to evade their attacks. But frogs can also easily get caught in a spider's web while jumping through the trees.  
* And once stuck, a spider can pierce through the frog's paper thin defenses like nothing, resulting in immediate paralysis. A highly volatile matchup and surprisingly neutral given the amphibian's low tier status. Beetles are an interesting case. They are flying insects, but because of their absurdly high power and defense stats, they're a challenge for spiders to overcome, even when caught in their web.  
* Their armor is so thick that a spider's fangs might not be able to pierce through. And they're so strong that even when fully enveloped in a web cocoon, they can sometimes tear through. Spider players are often stuck just sitting and waiting for beetles to either free themselves or die of exhaustion.  
* Birds are typically seen as the worst matchup for all terrestrial arthropods, but at the same time, silk web traps are highly effective against flying enemies, and birds are no exception. Webs are strong enough to capture small birds, and feathers offer very little protection against venomous fangs. However, if given the chance, birds will pick off an out- of-position spider in an instant, and the bird's armored beak presents no opportunity for the spider to counterattack.  
* Taking out a spider is worth a lot of XP for birds, as they can also steal all of the insects that are trapped in its web. One of the spider's worst matchups is against bats, which is surprising because it's another flyer. This is because the spider's double team technique has to be used on reaction. So, if a spider ever fails to see an enemy coming, it could easily get one-shot before it has a chance to respond.  
* This makes them especially vulnerable to builds with the echolocation ability, as they can strike from total darkness, giving the spider zero chance to detect the incoming attack before it's too late. And lastly, this isn't a matchup per se, but one of the other weaknesses spiders have are storms. Severe weather events affect web building spiders much more negatively than most characters.  
* As high winds and heavy precipitation can render a web ineffective if it doesn't just outright destroy it. All this to say, if you take away silk spinning, spiders are actually a real mixed bag when it comes to their viability in the meta. But with access to spinettes, many of them jump from low mid tier to high tier with jumping spiders in particular being arguably top tier. Silk really is just that broken.  
* Knowledge of the enemy's weak points and vulnerabilities is crucial for survival. This is true not just for spiders, but also for mechanized warfare. To see what I mean, you got to check out War Thunder, the most comprehensive vehicle combat game ever made. Free now on mobile, desktop, and consoles. War Thunder is truly one of the coolest games of all time, as they let you pilot over 2500 of history's most powerful war machines.  
* from biplanes and armored cars to fighter jets and battle tanks, each of which are just insanely detailed. In most combat games, combat kind of just boils down to clicking on the enemy and watching their health bar drain. But in War Thunder, this couldn't be further from the truth. Each vehicle is intricately modeled down to its individual components.  
* Meaning that if you know the ins and outs of the enemy, you can royally wreck house by targeting specific weak points like engines and fuel tanks. The game even shows you exactly where the damage is with its awesome X-ray view system, so you can learn and fight at the same time. The level of detail is truly unparalleled, not just for vehicle models, but for sound effects and environmental designs, too.  
* So, don't just sit on the sidelines. Join over 70 million other players in epic PvP battles today, and delve into the breathtaking experience that is War Thunder. It might be hard to believe, but somehow, even with this unmatched treasure trove of nearly endless content, this game is still somehow free. You can get it on PC, Xbox, PlayStation, and mobile devices.  
* Sign up using the links in my pinned comment or video description, and you'll even get a massive bonus pack for both new and returning players that haven't played for at least 6 months for PC and consoles, which comes with exclusive vehicle decorations, 100,000 Silver Lions, boosted experience, and a bunch of other goodies.  
* This offer is only available for a limited time, so don't delay. Check out the action-packed experience of War Thunder today. Thanks again for watching, and as always, good luck out there. \[Music\] Heat. Heat.

# 탭 35

* The overworld is full of unique biomes each with their own individual meta with contenders constantly vying for the top spot Today, we'll look at one of the most well known biomes: the African watering hole Just about every African player must traverse or interact with this biome even if their preferred server is the Savanna or even the desert.  
* Making this a hot zone for PVP And while there are some strong contenders from high-level builds like lions, elephants and monkeys Today we'll compare the two aquatic front runners: the crocodile and the hippopotamus. To determine which is truly top tier. One thing that immediately jumps out at anyone who runs into these builds Is their extremely high Vitality.  
* Both builds maximize the damage they can take in different extremely effective ways The Hippo simply has extremely high base stats for hit points Thick skin and high body fat allow the hippo to simply shrug off most hits on the opposite side of the spectrum We have the crocodile with only a moderate amount of base hit points however the Crocodile's thick scaly hide offers even more protection than the hippos blubber as an added bonus, crocodiles opted to spec for high HP regeneration As long as a crocodile survives a fight and can still bite and swim, it will invariably recover  
* Let's move on to their offensive stats The Crocodile build invests a huge amount of Ep into the bite attack this might seem foolish since typically most builds opt to have at least a few other attack moves at their disposal However in this case it appears that over investment into bite pushed its DPS so far beyond What the armor of a normal build can defend against the lack of other options in the neutral doesn't matter The utility of the bite move doesn't end there however Because it does so much damage it also has high hit stun  
* Allowing it to true combo into one of the most powerful attacks found in the entirety of the animal Kingdom: the death roll Unparalleled in DPS, This simple combo can end up one-hit killing all but the tankiest of builds Shifting over to the hippo.  
* We do have to keep in mind that this is a tank build, not a DPS build This means that its abilities lie much more in area-denial than scoring kills And you'd be hard-pressed to find a build more successful at area-denial than the hippopotamus The hippo's primary offensive move is charge which doesn't even need to connect in order to accomplish its goal Since simply casting the ability reduces its target's resolve astronomically This effect is strong enough to send even top tiers running for the hills and conceding the match without even taking a hit However, let's not pretend that the hippo charge is only for show  
* when this move connects it is absolutely Catastrophic its damage is Rivaled only by the rhinoceros and elephant But hippos can use charge just as effectively in the water Both the crocodile and hippo are hard countered by the elephant due to its sheer size and generally just try to avoid the match up It's no surprise that the most powerful kill combo in the world is held by one of the oldest builds in the meta There are hardly any builds from the time of the first crocs that are still relevant in the meta today  
* but the croc has seen no trouble being relevant in the meta across several expansions The hippo is much newer on the block which means it could be that the meta hasn't adapted and will eventually bounce back to counter the hippo or it could mean that the hippo hasn't even been fully optimized and could have an even higher rating once its player base gets closer to the skill ceiling I'm not going to definitively say which one ranks higher on the tier list since the meta is still evolving both are obviously top-tier in the watering hole biome  
* I would say that the croc is higher in the water while the hippo can hold its own against bigger challengers on land Thank you for watching and if you have a build that you'd like me to analyze The overworld is full of unique biomes each with their own individual meta with contenders constantly vying for the top spot Today, we'll look at one of the most well known biomes: the African watering hole Just about every African player must traverse or interact with this biome even if their preferred server is the Savanna or even the desert.  
* Making this a hot zone for PVP And while there are some strong contenders from high-level builds like lions, elephants and monkeys Today we'll compare the two aquatic front runners: the crocodile and the hippopotamus. To determine which is truly top tier. One thing that immediately jumps out at anyone who runs into these builds Is their extremely high Vitality.  
* Both builds maximize the damage they can take in different extremely effective ways The Hippo simply has extremely high base stats for hit points Thick skin and high body fat allow the hippo to simply shrug off most hits on the opposite side of the spectrum We have the crocodile with only a moderate amount of base hit points however the Crocodile's thick scaly hide offers even more protection than the hippos blubber as an added bonus, crocodiles opted to spec for high HP regeneration As long as a crocodile survives a fight and can still bite and swim, it will invariably recover  
* Let's move on to their offensive stats The Crocodile build invests a huge amount of Ep into the bite attack this might seem foolish since typically most builds opt to have at least a few other attack moves at their disposal However in this case it appears that over investment into bite pushed its DPS so far beyond What the armor of a normal build can defend against the lack of other options in the neutral doesn't matter The utility of the bite move doesn't end there however Because it does so much damage it also has high hit stun  
* Allowing it to true combo into one of the most powerful attacks found in the entirety of the animal Kingdom: the death roll Unparalleled in DPS, This simple combo can end up one-hit killing all but the tankiest of builds Shifting over to the hippo.  
* We do have to keep in mind that this is a tank build, not a DPS build This means that its abilities lie much more in area-denial than scoring kills And you'd be hard-pressed to find a build more successful at area-denial than the hippopotamus The hippo's primary offensive move is charge which doesn't even need to connect in order to accomplish its goal Since simply casting the ability reduces its target's resolve astronomically This effect is strong enough to send even top tiers running for the hills and conceding the match without even taking a hit However, let's not pretend that the hippo charge is only for show  
* when this move connects it is absolutely Catastrophic its damage is Rivaled only by the rhinoceros and elephant But hippos can use charge just as effectively in the water Both the crocodile and hippo are hard countered by the elephant due to its sheer size and generally just try to avoid the match up It's no surprise that the most powerful kill combo in the world is held by one of the oldest builds in the meta There are hardly any builds from the time of the first crocs that are still relevant in the meta today  
* but the croc has seen no trouble being relevant in the meta across several expansions The hippo is much newer on the block which means it could be that the meta hasn't adapted and will eventually bounce back to counter the hippo or it could mean that the hippo hasn't even been fully optimized and could have an even higher rating once its player base gets closer to the skill ceiling I'm not going to definitively say which one ranks higher on the tier list since the meta is still evolving both are obviously top-tier in the watering hole biome  
* I would say that the croc is higher in the water while the hippo can hold its own against bigger challengers on land Thank you for watching and if you have a build that you'd like me to analyze

* \[Music\] the meta as we know it today is built around support classes for every matchup between fighter classes there is a web of other connections between fighter and support popular examples include sea anemones and clownfish leafcutter ants and fungus cleaner shrimp and fish and many more but the most dominant support classes are the ones human players can ally themselves with a lot of different builds have fought for the spot and in some instances even held the number one spot such as horses camels and Falcons but  
* there's really no question that the two frontrunners in this category are the cat and the dog so today I'll explain what unique characteristics make these builds the supports of choice for the current undisputed number one on the tier list so I'm not really gonna beat around the bush here the best support class in the game is the dog the base dog build without leveling up or specialising already has access to the most powerful morale-boosting aura ever seen across any of the expansions or servers this can completely nullify  
* buffs such as depression and stress or loneliness and can affect any player not just humans this powerful ability is available immediately at the start of your game and lasts your entire playthrough now to be fair cats have this ability too and this alone would already make any build a high tier support but when it comes to dogs this is just icing on the cake when it comes to their effectiveness as a teammate aside from their massive morale boost higher level dogs can also mitigate the blindness debuff crowd control block  
* attacks grab targets to start team combos track down loot quests objectives or players intimidate intruders or aggressors and many more incredible feats so at this point you're probably wondering how is there even a debate between cat and dog mains as to which one is truly the best support and the answer is that cats aren't a support class at all we've talked about cats in the meta before and how their top tier stealth assassin classes while dog players were already accustomed to team-based play and had no trouble speaking into support abilities  
* once they decided to switch roles from pack under to support cats never actually made this switch instead what granted them access to the human's territory was the cat players own support class a microorganism known as Toxoplasma gondii this microbe has its own AOE but instead of a morale-boosting aura effect like dogs have Toxoplasma Zora inflicts a mental status effect that reduces fear before humans rose to the top spot in the meta this status effect was mostly used on rodents making the prey found in a cat's territory  
* easier to take out since there were less likely to evade predators but now this is used on humans to manipulate them into allowing cats to share their territory in return Toxoplasma players gain tons of easy XP by causing tons of infections among any mammal in the cat's territory it's a conspiracy don't believe me consider that as many as 50% of all humans have been infected by Toxoplasma and that the drug used to treat toxoplasmosis has recently spiked in price over 5000 percent making it almost impossible to stop Toxoplasma  
* players from doing their thing and the end result here is that cats have essentially stolen a top-tier spot in the meta and use it to grief weaker builds like songbirds and mice while they're truly talked to your comrades the humans and dogs are none the wiser so here's my advice for playing these classes for dog players the most important thing to do is to try to get all the bonus specializations you can even though you're already a powerhouse when it comes to your abilities and base stats to truly reach the upper limit of  
* your potential you're going to want to try to unlock one of the prestige classes such as a hunting dog police dog or seeing-eye dog one thing you need to watch out for is that too many playthroughs as a moral support class can start to lead to the problem where human players try to speck your build for you a lot of times they'll put too many points into aesthetics sometimes at the cost of your other stats other times they may even accidentally cause it to roll over into a negative if you play cat there are three play  
* styles available outside inside and stray to maximize potential ambush opportunities and minimize risk you're going to want to go for the outside cat play style which combines the security for new human allies with the freedom of not having territory boundaries this type of build allows you to reach an absurdly high kdr they're so oppressive on lightweight builds like birds and mice that a score about 24 billion kills a year on them in my opinion this is grounds enough for a nerf but the devs haven't been too active with balance  
* patches lately so I wouldn't hold your breath thanks for watching if you've got a top and you'd like to see covered on this channel be sure to leave a comment or head on over to patreon comm slash tears you to join the ongoing discussion on the tier list be sure to go back and watch my video discussing the to your list of cats that don't abuse mind controlling parasites to carve out their niche and don't forget subscribe so you don't miss the next video

* this episode is sponsored by brilliant there was a lot of debate as to whether or not the rumor balance patch 1.3 point seven was actually coming but as of the IPCC climate report we now not only have confirmation that the update is real but we now know the precise details concerning which play styles are getting buffed and which ones are getting nerfed so we're gonna go through built by build and discuss how they've been buffed or nerfed by this update polar bears can no longer rely on their land-based movement  
* abilities to get around with ice sheets becoming more fragmented putting points into swimming and diving will become a necessity in order to survive ice sheets were a protective barrier that prevented orcas from invading the breeding grounds of seals and narwhals because these ice sheets are melting orcas will be able to wreak havoc on your sheets we now have a lot less defensive options but if you're an orca main be careful because the passages into these waters open and close frequently meaning it's possible to  
* become trapped cephalopods like the octopus and squid are able to grow and respawn much more effectively in warmer water the cephalopod playerbase has seen explosive growth lately as their highly adaptive play style has allowed them to easily move into territory that they couldn't previously due to the water being too cold in the past I've counted their short lifespans against them because it limits how much time they have to learn advanced techniques with their high intelligence but in this case their short lifespan and quick respawn  
* time is a huge benefit accelerating how quickly they can adapt to changes in the environment sea turtles are affected in two ways first because they can only respawn on beaches the rising water level means their spawn points are becoming smaller and much more crowded second because they're reptiles their gender is not dictated by RNG but by temperature the higher temperatures have led to a sharp spike in the number of players spawning in as females while polar bears are being driven south grizzly bears are being driven north  
* this has led to the creation of a new build the growler bear which is essentially an intermediate between the two that's more powerful than the grizzly there but also not limited to the Arctic like polar bears our house cats are mostly unaffected by climate change although the warmer climate allows cats to respawn at any time instead of only in the spring on the other hand fleas and ticks also multiply faster so make sure you have at least one human player in your party so that they can heal you from the diseases that these parasites  
* can cause jellyfish are perfectly suited to living in a warmer more acidic ocean like cephalopods they've seen explosive growth but to an even greater extreme the box jellyfish and Irukandji in particular are quickly becoming the most oppressive build in the meadow in some cases even damaging battleships it's been over five hundred million years since the last time jellyfish were top-tier so I see this as a fair balanced change increased overall temperature has different effects in different places in some areas it means  
* more water and in some places like the African savannah it means a lot less ticks in Africa thrive in drought conditions which is terrible news for mammal builds playing on that server in particular lions wildebeest and antelope can expect to be playing with reduced max HP because they'll constantly be being drained by tech players in the Midwest tech populations are surging as well which has been disastrous for moose and deer populations as well as a certain youtuber who got Lyme disease not a ton to say here except that the increased  
* temperature expands their range a bit since the areas that were previously too cold are now habitable this applies to sea snakes too one of the other new changes in this update is an increase in severe weather event frequency and severity this to me seems like a change meant to directly nerf humans because severe weather deals bonus damage to structures weather is one of the few things in the game that humans can't actually fight and while they can simply flee just like any other build their structures are helpless in  
* addition to this the rising sea level would force many human players have to find a new home server permafrost the Arctic servers signature debuff that prevents trees from spawning there has been removed in a lot of areas this means our boreal builds are gaining a ton of traction much further north than before the most important of which is the goshawk which has phenomenal matchups against the small mammals of the Arctic similar to snakes scorpions have the ability to enter more temperate zones now in fact let's just get this  
* out of the way this is true for most arthropod builds that play on the Central American servers so fire ants and killer bees are moving north as well \[Music\] for most players the acidification of the ocean is problematic but it doesn't actually deal any direct damage to them however for bivalves as well as for just about any bill that puts points into a shell the acidic ocean directly corrodes their Armour damaging it and drastically lowering the player's defense level which is pretty much the only thing they  
* have going for them \[Music\] most Tigers live near the coast which is the region most threatened by climate change along with the Arctic Tigers rely on coastal freshwater springs in order to survive but the rising sea levels are causing some of these streams to mix with saltwater the increased rainfall in the northern Servers has vastly increased spawn rates for mosquitoes which require standing water to respond this is a problem for Crowe players because the West Nile debuff mosquito spread deals bonus damage to crows one  
* of this builds main advantages is his ability to move through deep snow without receiving a speed penalty but with snowfall being reduced and winters being shorter this advantage is becoming less useful so Wolverine players may want to invest those points somewhere else the increased rainfall and temperature has accelerated the spread of chytrid fungus an infection that directly targets and phibian and is one of the deadliest plagues in the history of the game ultimately this only accelerates the inevitable but yeah and

# 탭 38

* There are some really awesome moves in the game that a lot of players end up unlocking Tackle, slash, thrash, stomp, double-edge, etc. These attacks are all really great techniques that can be viable during your entire playthrough However, they all have one thing in common: they're all normal type. And this isn't a bad thing.  
* The vast majority of the most viable builds in the meta got to where they are because of how good their normal-type moves are But today we're going to push past the normal-type moves and analyze the other possibilities that exist: the elemental moves. While they take a huge amount of evolution points to spec into, the meta right now is pretty much centered around normal-type moves And as a result, resistance to elemental-type attacks is generally almost nonexistent With that said, let's get into it So probably the most popular example of this is the electric eel  
* Just like its name implies, it forgoes the popular choices of fins, teeth, and, well, really any aesthetics at all And sinks all of its evolution points into a single, signature move: Discharge This electric-type attack is a short-range AoE burst move that can be used both offensively and defensively It's damage scales inversely with the target's size So while it can insta-gib entire parties of fish, most larger builds will walk away with only minimal HP loss That is, unless it crits and prompts cardiac arrest or seizure  
* Since this move can be used both on offense and defense, And has almost no counter-play besides just tanking it and trying to escape I rank the electric eel as A tier Held back from S Tier only by its low intelligence and low mobility due to lack of strong fins Next up, we have a group of fish players who traded HP, strength, and defense For one of the only range attacks in the game: the archerfish Archerfish can use the move watergun; This move doesn't do much damage, but it has a high chance of knocking small targets off their feet,  
* Which can, of course, combo straight into pretty much anything In order to actually spec into this move, not only do these players need to invest in the structural evolution required to cast this ability, But they also had to greatly increase their intelligence in order to be able to aim their shots Connecting a hit with this move requires you to intuit how far away your target is How much force is needed to knock it down, And also gauge how much distortion effect of light refraction While this is all very impressive, personally I don't see archerfish as exceptionally viable in the meta  
* For a few reasons Most importantly, this ability has no defensive use in a meta where defense is extremely important Furthermore, this ability requires a player to camp at the surface of the water Which is precisely where you don't want to be If you're playing on the same server as eagles, hawks, and other high-tier aerial builds With all this considered, I'd put the archerfish at the top of C Tier Ranged attacks are extremely effective, but you need a solid foundation for them to really excel If you're looking for a good ranged ability, be sure to watch my video on optimizing snakes  
* Or if you're picky enough that you want an aquatic build with ranged or elemental abilities, Definitely research the orca A build so incredibly overpowered that I'm just going to need to make an entirely seprarate video on them The last build that I want to talk about today possesses an explosive fire-type move The bombardier beetle This is an example of sometimes there are abilites that are worth going through an entire skill tree of boring and weak abilities just to get to In this case, the beetle players who unlock this ability had to spec deep into the chemistry tree  
* Which is pretty much all composed of gross and unappealing powers like skunk smells and anti-debuff agents Most insect players do spend a few points here and unlock 1 or 2 abilities in this tree However, if you unlock enough of these at the same time, They can be simultaneously used to produce a completely different move One of the only fire-type moves available in the entire game: Fire blast This can, of course, inflict a burn, which both does damage and inflicts its target with a serious debuff To many important stat modifiers like the immune system  
* But really, all of those effects are just icing on the cake All these players really need this ability for is to escape disadvantageous situations Either by blinding the enemy, or causing them to flinch so they miss their chance to attack Since beetles are already minimum B Tier, I would say that this excellent defensive move firmly puts them in A Tier Kept out of S Tier because they still lack the most powerful insect-exclusive ability in the game which I'll cover in another video Anyways, in summary, elemental-type attacks are costly  
* So you really need to weigh your options and plan out your build in advance before you attempt them New players are almost always better off sticking to good all-around builds Like the bear, iguana, and boar But if you're clever about it, you can really get some amazing gameplay out of these unique abilities
